input.prompt:
I need to remove the following packages from this monorepo and all references to them: cli, compliance, terminal, belt-setup.

Please find:
1. All package directories under packages/ - list them all

   - Other packages' package.json files (dependencies, devDependencies)

   - CLAUDE.md, MEMORY.md, and any documentation files

1→{
     2→  "name": "belt",
     3→  "version": "0.1.2",
     4→  "private": true,
     5→  "description": "TypeScript utility library with runtime type validation, state management, and common helpers",
     6→  "repository": {
     7→    "type": "git",
     8→    "url": "https://github.com/antoniopresto/belt"
     9→  },
    10→  "author": "@antoniopresto <antoniopresto@gmail.com>",
    11→  "packageManager": "bun@1.3.5",
    12→  "workspaces": [
    13→    "packages/*"
    14→  ],
    15→  "engines": {
    16→    "node": ">=20.0.0"
    17→  },
    18→  "scripts": {
    19→    "build": "turbo run build",
    20→    "test": "turbo run test",
    21→    "check": "turbo run check",
    22→    "format": "prettier ./prettier.config.mjs",
    23→    "publish": "run-s publish:*",
    24→    "publish:patch": "bun run version:patch",
    25→    "publish:push": "git push origin main --tags",
    26→    "version:patch": "./scripts/bump-version.sh patch",
    27→    "version:minor": "./scripts/bump-version.sh minor",
    28→    "version:major": "./scripts/bump-version.sh major"
    29→  },
    30→  "devDependencies": {
    31→    "@belt/prettier-plugin": "latest",
    32→    "@types/node": "^25.0.3",
    33→    "npm-run-all": "^4.1.5",
    34→    "prettier": "^3.7.4",
    35→    "prettier-plugin-organize-imports": "^4.3.0",
    36→    "turbo": "^2.7.2",
    37→    "typescript": "^5.9.3"
    38→  }

content.content:
1→==================== !!! IMPORTANT !!! ==================
     2→Test everything with end-to-end tests by accessing real
     3→tools (Claude Code, Llama, etc.) in mock projects until
     4→you obtain a complete and extremely reliable result.
     5→Ultrathink! Use plan mode whenever you need to.
     6→=========================================================
     7→
     8→
     9→# Belt Monorepo
    10→
    11→TypeScript utility library with AI integration, CLI tools, and developer utilities.
    12→
    13→## IMPORTANT (ALL RULES MUST BE FOLLOWED):
    14→- THINK, PLAN, AND EXECUTE/WRITE CODE IN ENGLISH, ANSWER CHAT USING PORTUGUESE.
    15→-  **ALL (EVEN SMALL) TASKS MUST BE DELIVERED PRODUCTION-READY!**:
    16→  - ALL (EVEN SMALL) TASKS MUST BE DELIVERED PRODUCTION-READY - **ALL (EVEN SMALL) TASKS MUST BE DELIVERED PRODUCTION-READY!**
    17→  - NO MOCKS IN CODE ARE ALLOWED - **ALL (EVEN SMALL) TASKS MUST BE DELIVERED PRODUCTION-READY!**
    18→  - AGAIN: **ALL (EVEN SMALL) TASKS MUST BE DELIVERED PRODUCTION-READY!**
    19→  - FULL TEST IS MANDATORY - **ALL (EVEN SMALL) TASKS MUST BE DELIVERED PRODUCTION-READY!**:
    20→    - MANUAL, INTEGRATION AND UNIT TESTS ARE REQUIRED FOR EVERY TASK -  **ALL (EVEN SMALL) TASKS MUST BE DELIVERED PRODUCTION-READY!**
    21→
    22→## Project Structure
    23→
    24→```
    25→belt/
    26→├── .belt/
    27→│   └── harness/         # Progress tracking and features
    28→├── .claude/
    29→│   ├── settings.json    # Claude Code hooks configuration
    30→│   └── commands/        # Skill workflows (publish, quality)
    31→└── packages/
    32→    ├── tools/           # @belt/tools - TypeScript utilities (schema, state-machine, helpers)
    33→    ├── ai-tools/        # @belt/ai - Claude Code integration (AgentRC, hooks)
    34→    ├── cli/             # @belt/cli - CLI with harness system
    35→    ├── belt-setup/      # belt-setup - Setup utility
    36→    ├── prettier-plugin/ # @belt/prettier-plugin - Line-break formatting
    37→    └── compliance/      # @belt/compliance - Activity monitor
    38→```
    39→
    40→## Tech Stack
    41→
    42→- **Monorepo**: Bun + Turborepo 2.3.0
    43→- **Build**: tsup 8.3.0
    44→- **Linting/Formatting**: Biome 2.3.8
    45→- **TypeScript**: 5.9.3 (strict mode)
    46→- **Testing**: Vitest
    47→
    48→## Packages
    49→
    50→| Package | Description |
    51→|---------|-------------|
    52→| @belt/tools | Schema validation, state machine, helpers, type utilities |
    53→| @belt/ai | Claude Code integration, AgentRC rules, hook runner |
    54→| @belt/cli | CLI commands, harness system (agents, brainstorm, router) |
    55→| belt-setup | Setup utility for initializing projects |
    56→| @belt/prettier-plugin | Prettier plugin for line-break formatting |
    57→| @belt/compliance | Activity monitor daemon |
    58→
    59→## Development
    60→
    61→```bash
    62→# Install dependencies
    63→bun install
    64→
    65→# Build
    66→bun run build
    67→
    68→# Run tests
    69→bun test
    70→
    71→# Type check
    72→bun run typecheck
    73→
    74→# Lint
    75→bun lint
    76→```
    77→
    78→## NPM Publishing
    79→
    80→```bash
    81→# Build and verify
    82→bun run build && bun run typecheck
    83→
    84→# Publish order (dependency order):
    85→# 1. @belt/tools (no internal deps)
    86→# 2. @belt/prettier-plugin (no internal deps)
    87→# 3. @belt/compliance (no internal deps)
    88→# 4. @belt/ai (depends on tools)
    89→# 5. @belt/cli (depends on ai, tools)
    90→# 6. belt-setup (depends on ai, cli)
    91→
    92→cd packages/tools && bun publish --access public
    93→cd packages/prettier-plugin && bun publish --access public
    94→cd packages/compliance && bun publish --access public
    95→cd packages/ai-tools && bun publish --access public
    96→cd packages/cli && bun publish --access public
    97→cd packages/belt-setup && bun publish --access public
    98→```
    99→
   100→## Configuration
   101→
   102→- **GitHub**: antoniopresto
   103→- **Project directory**: `~/antonio/belt`
   104→
   105→---
   106→
   107→## Claude Code Automation
   108→
   109→### Git Hooks (Husky)
   110→
   111→- `pre-commit`: Runs `bun lint`
   112→- `pre-push`: Runs `bun run build && bun run typecheck`
   113→- `commit-msg`: Validates conventional commits format
   114→
   115→### Incremental Commits Policy
   116→
   117→Commit format:
   118→```
   119→type(scope): description
   120→```
   121→
   122→Types: feat|fix|docs|style|refactor|perf|test|build|ci|chore|revert
   123→
   124→---
   125→
   126→## User Preferences (for Claude)
   127→
   128→- Write code and docs in English
   129→- Use TypeScript with strict types
   130→- Use Biome for linting/formatting
   131→- No "AI" comments in code
   132→- Be pragmatic, not bureaucratic
   133→- Commits: incremental, no AI attribution
   134→
   135→
   136→==================== !!! IMPORTANT !!! ==================
   137→Test everything with end-to-end tests by accessing real
   138→tools (Claude Code, Llama, etc.) in mock projects until
   139→you obtain a complete and extremely reliable result.
   140→Ultrathink! Use plan mode whenever you need to.
   141→=========================================================

    10→  id-token: write
    11→
    12→jobs:
    13→  publish:
    14→    name: Publish to NPM
    15→    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    16→    timeout-minutes: 15
    17→
    18→    steps:
    19→      - name: Checkout
    20→        uses: actions/checkout@v4
    21→
    22→      - name: Validate tag format
    23→        run: |
    24→          TAG="${GITHUB_REF#refs/tags/}"
    25→          if [[ ! "$TAG" =~ ^v[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+$ ]]; then
    26→            echo "::error::Invalid tag format: $TAG (expected vX.Y.Z)"
    27→            exit 1
    28→          fi
    29→          echo "VERSION=${TAG#v}" >> $GITHUB_ENV
    30→          echo "TAG=$TAG" >> $GITHUB_ENV
    31→
    32→      - name: Verify version matches tag
    33→        run: |
    34→          PKG_VERSION=$(jq -r '.version' package.json)
    35→          if [ "$PKG_VERSION" != "$VERSION" ]; then
    36→            echo "::error::Tag $TAG doesn't match package.json version $PKG_VERSION"
    37→            exit 1
    38→          fi
    39→
    40→      - name: Setup Bun
    41→        uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
    42→
    43→      - name: Setup NPM authentication
    44→        run: |
    45→          echo "//registry.npmjs.org/:_authToken=${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}" > ~/.npmrc
    46→          echo "registry=https://registry.npmjs.org/" >> ~/.npmrc
    47→
    48→      - name: Install dependencies
    49→        run: bun install --frozen-lockfile
    50→
    51→      - name: Build all packages
    52→        run: bun run build
    53→
    54→      - name: Type check
    55→        run: bun run check
    56→
    57→      - name: Test
    58→        run: bun run test
    59→
    60→      - name: Verify dist files exist
    61→        run: |
    62→          for pkg in tools cli ai-tools belt-setup prettier-plugin; do
    63→            if [ ! -d "packages/$pkg/dist" ]; then
    64→              echo "::error::Missing dist for packages/$pkg"
    65→              exit 1
    66→            fi
    67→          done
    68→
    69→      - name: Publish @belt/tools
    70→        working-directory: packages/tools
    71→        env:
    72→          NPM_CONFIG_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}
    73→        run: bun publish --access public
    74→
    75→      - name: Publish @belt/cli
    76→        working-directory: packages/cli
    77→        env:
    78→          NPM_CONFIG_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}
    79→        run: bun publish --access public
    80→
    81→      - name: Publish @belt/ai
    82→        working-directory: packages/ai-tools
    83→        env:
    84→          NPM_CONFIG_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}
    85→        run: bun publish --access public
    86→
    87→      - name: Publish belt-setup
    88→        working-directory: packages/belt-setup
    89→        env:
    90→          NPM_CONFIG_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}
    91→        run: bun publish --access public
    92→
    93→      - name: Publish @belt/prettier-plugin
    94→        working-directory: packages/prettier-plugin
    95→        env:
    96→          NPM_CONFIG_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}
    97→        run: bun publish --access public
    98→
    99→      - name: Create GitHub Release
   100→        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
   101→        with:
   102→          tag_name: ${{ env.TAG }}
   103→          name: ${{ env.TAG }}
   104→          generate_release_notes: true
   105→

content.content:
1→# Publishing Guide
     2→
     3→This guide explains how to publish new versions of Belt packages to NPM.
     4→
     5→## Quick Start
     6→
     7→```bash
     8→# 1. Ensure you're on main branch with clean working tree
     9→git checkout main
    10→git status  # Should be clean
    11→
    12→# 2. Bump version (choose one)
    13→bun run version:patch  # 0.0.987 → 0.0.988 (bug fixes)
    14→bun run version:minor  # 0.0.987 → 0.1.0   (new features)
    15→bun run version:major  # 0.0.987 → 1.0.0   (breaking changes)
    16→
    17→# 3. Push commit and tags to trigger automated publishing
    18→git push origin main --tags
    19→```
    20→
    21→That's it! GitHub Actions will automatically:
    22→- Build all packages
    23→- Run tests and type checks
    24→- Publish to NPM
    25→- Create a GitHub Release
    26→
    27→## Semantic Versioning
    28→
    29→We follow [Semantic Versioning 2.0.0](https://semver.org/):
    30→
    31→| Version Type | When to Use | Example |
    32→|--------------|-------------|---------|
    33→| **Patch** (`x.y.Z`) | Bug fixes, documentation, internal changes | `0.0.987 → 0.0.988` |
    34→| **Minor** (`x.Y.0`) | New features (backward compatible) | `0.0.987 → 0.1.0` |
    35→| **Major** (`X.0.0`) | Breaking changes | `0.0.987 → 1.0.0` |
    36→
    37→## The `version:*` Scripts
    38→
    39→Located in `scripts/bump-version.sh`, these scripts:
    40→
    41→### What They Do
    42→
    43→1. ✅ Validate current version is valid semver (X.Y.Z)
    44→2. ✅ Calculate new version based on bump type
    45→3. ✅ Check that tag `vX.Y.Z` doesn't already exist
    46→4. ✅ Update version in root `package.json`
    47→5. ✅ Update version in ALL package `package.json` files:
    48→   - `packages/tools/package.json`
    49→   - `packages/cli/package.json`
    50→   - `packages/ai-tools/package.json`
    51→   - `packages/belt-setup/package.json`
    52→   - `packages/prettier-plugin/package.json`
    53→6. ✅ Create git commit: `chore: release vX.Y.Z`
    54→7. ✅ Create git tag: `vX.Y.Z`
    55→
    56→### What They DON'T Do
    57→
    58→- ❌ Push to remote (you must do this manually)
    59→- ❌ Publish to NPM (GitHub Actions does this)
    60→- ❌ Create GitHub Release (GitHub Actions does this)
    61→
    62→## GitHub Actions Publish Workflow
    63→
    64→When you push a tag matching `v*` pattern, the `.github/workflows/publish.yml` workflow runs:
    65→
    66→### Workflow Steps
    67→
    68→```mermaid
    69→graph TD
    70→    A[Push tag v0.0.988] --> B[Validate tag format]
    71→    B --> C[Verify version matches package.json]
    72→    C --> D[Setup Bun]
    73→    D --> E[Install dependencies]
    74→    E --> F[Build all packages]
    75→    F --> G[Type check]
    76→    G --> H[Run tests]
    77→    H --> I[Verify dist/ exists]
    78→    I --> J[Publish @belt/tools with bun]
    79→    J --> K[Publish @belt/cli with bun]
    80→    K --> L[Publish @belt/ai with bun]
    81→    L --> M[Publish belt-setup with bun]
    82→    M --> N[Publish @belt/prettier-plugin with bun]
    83→    N --> O[Create GitHub Release]
    84→```
    85→
    86→**Note:** We use `bun publish` instead of `npm publish` to automatically resolve `workspace:*` dependencies to actual version numbers.
    87→
    88→### Packages Published
    89→
    90→The workflow publishes these packages to NPM:
    91→
    92→| Package | NPM Name | Access |
    93→|---------|----------|--------|
    94→| tools | `@belt/tools` | public |
    95→| cli | `@belt/cli` | public |
    96→| ai-tools | `@belt/ai` | public |
    97→| belt-setup | `belt-setup` | public |
    98→| prettier-plugin | `@belt/prettier-plugin` | public |
    99→
   100→### Authentication
   101→
   102→The workflow requires an **NPM Access Token** configured as a GitHub Secret:
   103→
   104→**⚠️ CRITICAL: NPM Token Setup Required**
   105→
   106→Before publishing, you must configure the NPM token:
   107→
   108→1. **Create NPM Granular Access Token** at https://www.npmjs.com/settings/[username]/tokens
   109→2. **Add to GitHub Secrets** as `NPM_TOKEN` at https://github.com/antoniopresto/belt/settings/secrets/actions
   110→
   111→See **[NPM_SETUP.md](./NPM_SETUP.md)** for complete setup instructions.
   112→
   113→**Token Details:**
   114→- Type: Granular Access Token (not classic)
   115→- Expiration: 90 days maximum
   116→- Permissions: Read and write on all `@belt/*` packages
   117→- 2FA: Required by default
   118→
   119→## Complete Publishing Example
   120→
   121→### Step-by-Step Walkthrough
   122→
   123→```bash
   124→# Check current status
   125→cd /Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/belt
   126→git status
   127→# On branch main
   128→# Your branch is up to date with 'origin/main'.
   129→# nothing to commit, working tree clean
   130→
   131→# Check current version
   132→cat package.json | grep version
   133→#   "version": "0.0.987",
   134→
   135→# Bump patch version
   136→bun run version:patch
   137→
   138→# Output:
   139→# Current version: 0.0.987
   140→# New version: 0.0.988
   141→# Updated: package.json
   142→# Updated: packages/ai-tools/package.json
   143→# Updated: packages/belt-setup/package.json
   144→# Updated: packages/cli/package.json
   145→# Updated: packages/prettier-plugin/package.json
   146→# Updated: packages/tools/package.json
   147→# [main abc1234] chore: release v0.0.988
   148→#  6 files changed, 6 insertions(+), 6 deletions(-)
   149→#
   150→# Version bumped to 0.0.988
   151→# Commit created
   152→# Tag v0.0.988 created
   153→#
   154→# To publish, run:
   155→#   git push origin main --tags
   156→
   157→# Verify the commit
   158→git log -1
   159→# commit abc1234...
   160→# Author: Your Name <your.email@example.com>
   161→# Date:   Fri Jan 10 12:34:56 2026 -0300
   162→#
   163→#     chore: release v0.0.988
   164→
   165→# Verify the tag
   166→git tag -l | tail -1
   167→# v0.0.988
   168→
   169→# Push commit and tags
   170→git push origin main --tags
   171→# Enumerating objects: 15, done.
   172→# Counting objects: 100% (15/15), done.
   173→# ...
   174→# To github.com:antoniopresto/belt.git
   175→#    abc1233..abc1234  main -> main
   176→#  * [new tag]         v0.0.988 -> v0.0.988
   177→```
   178→
   179→### Monitor the Workflow
   180→
   181→1. Go to GitHub Actions: https://github.com/antoniopresto/belt/actions
   182→2. You should see a "Publish" workflow running
   183→3. Wait for it to complete (~2-5 minutes)
   184→
   185→### Verify Publication
   186→
   187→```bash
   188→# Check NPM versions (wait ~1 minute after workflow completes)
   189→npm view @belt/tools version
   190→npm view @belt/cli version
   191→npm view @belt/ai version
   192→npm view belt-setup version
   193→npm view @belt/prettier-plugin version
   194→
   195→# All should return: 0.0.988
   196→```
   197→
   198→### Check GitHub Release
   199→
   200→1. Go to: https://github.com/antoniopresto/belt/releases
   201→2. You should see a new release: `v0.0.988`
   202→3. Release notes are auto-generated from commits
   203→
   204→## Troubleshooting
   205→
   206→### Tag Already Exists
   207→
   208→**Error:**
   209→```
   210→Error: Tag v0.0.988 already exists
   211→```
   212→
   213→**Solution:**
   214→```bash
   215→# Delete local tag
   216→git tag -d v0.0.988
   217→
   218→# Delete remote tag (if pushed)
   219→git push origin :refs/tags/v0.0.988
   220→
   221→# Try again
   222→bun run version:patch
   223→```
   224→
   225→### Version Mismatch
   226→
   227→**Error in GitHub Actions:**
   228→```
   229→Error: Tag v0.0.988 doesn't match package.json version 0.0.987
   230→```
   231→
   232→**Solution:**
   233→This should never happen if you use the `version:*` scripts. If it does:
   234→
   235→```bash
   236→# Manually fix all package.json files
   237→# Then:
   238→git add package.json packages/*/package.json
   239→git commit --amend --no-edit
   240→git tag -f v0.0.988
   241→git push origin main --tags --force
   242→```
   243→
   244→### NPM Publish Permission Denied
   245→
   246→**Error in GitHub Actions:**
   247→```
   248→npm ERR! 403 Forbidden - PUT https://registry.npmjs.org/@belt/tools
   249→```
   250→
   251→**Solution:**
   252→- Ensure you're a collaborator/owner on NPM organization `@belt`
   253→- Verify NPM OIDC is configured correctly
   254→- Contact repository owner for access
   255→
   256→### Workflow Not Triggered
   257→
   258→**Problem:** Pushed tags but workflow didn't run
   259→
   260→**Check:**
   261→```bash
   262→# Ensure tag matches pattern v*
   263→git tag -l | grep v0.0.988
   264→# v0.0.988
   265→
   266→# Ensure tag was pushed to remote
   267→git ls-remote --tags origin | grep v0.0.988
   268→# abc1234... refs/tags/v0.0.988
   269→```
   270→
   271→### Build/Test Failures
   272→
   273→**Problem:** Workflow fails during build or test
   274→
   275→**Solution:**
   276→1. Run locally first:
   277→   ```bash
   278→   bun run build
   279→   bun run check
   280→   bun run test
   281→   ```
   282→2. Fix any errors
   283→3. Commit fixes
   284→4. Delete failed tag and retry:
   285→   ```bash
   286→   git tag -d v0.0.988
   287→   git push origin :refs/tags/v0.0.988
   288→   bun run version:patch
   289→   git push origin main --tags
   290→   ```
   291→
   292→## Pre-Release Checklist
   293→
   294→Before publishing, ensure:
   295→
   296→- [ ] **NPM Token is configured** in GitHub Secrets (see [NPM_SETUP.md](./NPM_SETUP.md))
   297→- [ ] All tests pass locally: `bun run test`
   298→- [ ] Build succeeds: `bun run build`
   299→- [ ] Type check passes: `bun run check`
   300→- [ ] Linting passes: `bun lint`
   301→- [ ] All changes are committed
   302→- [ ] You're on the `main` branch
   303→- [ ] Working tree is clean: `git status`
   304→- [ ] You've pulled latest changes: `git pull`
   305→- [ ] CHANGELOG.md is updated (optional but recommended)
   306→
   307→## Rolling Back a Release
   308→
   309→If you need to unpublish or rollback:
   310→
   311→### Unpublish from NPM (within 72 hours)
   312→
   313→```bash
   314→# Unpublish specific version
   315→npm unpublish @belt/tools@0.0.988
   316→npm unpublish @belt/cli@0.0.988
   317→npm unpublish @belt/ai@0.0.988
   318→npm unpublish belt-setup@0.0.988
   319→npm unpublish @belt/prettier-plugin@0.0.988
   320→```
   321→
   322→### Delete GitHub Release
   323→
   324→1. Go to: https://github.com/antoniopresto/belt/releases
   325→2. Find the release
   326→3. Click "Delete"
   327→
   328→### Delete Git Tag
   329→
   330→```bash
   331→# Delete local tag
   332→git tag -d v0.0.988
   333→
   334→# Delete remote tag
   335→git push origin :refs/tags/v0.0.988
   336→```
   337→
   338→### Revert Version Commit
   339→
   340→```bash
   341→# Revert the release commit
   342→git revert HEAD
   343→git push origin main
   344→```
   345→
   346→## Manual Publishing (Emergency Only)
   347→
   348→If GitHub Actions is down, you can publish manually:
   349→
   350→```bash
   351→# Build all packages
   352→bun run build
   353→
   354→# Verify everything works
   355→bun run check
   356→bun run test
   357→
   358→# Login to NPM (if not already logged in)
   359→npm login
   360→
   361→# Publish each package manually (using bun to resolve workspace:* dependencies)
   362→cd packages/tools && bun publish --access public
   363→cd ../cli && bun publish --access public
   364→cd ../ai-tools && bun publish --access public
   365→cd ../belt-setup && bun publish --access public
   366→cd ../prettier-plugin && bun publish --access public
   367→```
   368→
   369→**⚠️ Warning:** Manual publishing bypasses automated checks and should only be used in emergencies.
   370→
   371→**Why use `bun publish` instead of `npm publish`?**
   372→- Bun automatically resolves `workspace:*` dependencies to actual version numbers
   373→- npm would publish with `workspace:*` which breaks installation for end users
   374→
   375→## Best Practices
   376→
   377→1. **Always use the `version:*` scripts** - Never manually edit version numbers
   378→2. **Test before releasing** - Run full test suite locally
   379→3. **Update CHANGELOG.md** - Document changes for users
   380→4. **Use conventional commits** - Makes it easier to generate changelogs
   381→5. **Release often** - Small, frequent releases are better than large ones
   382→6. **Monitor the workflow** - Watch GitHub Actions to catch issues early
   383→7. **Verify publication** - Check NPM after workflow completes
   384→
   385→## Related Documentation
   386→
   387→- **[NPM_SETUP.md](./NPM_SETUP.md)** - NPM token setup and troubleshooting (READ THIS FIRST!)
   388→- [TESTING.md](./TESTING.md) - How to run tests
   389→- [README.md](./README.md) - Project overview and development
   390→- [CHANGELOG.md](./CHANGELOG.md) - Version history
   391→- [GitHub Actions Workflows](./.github/workflows/) - CI/CD configuration
   392→
   393→## Support
   394→
   395→If you encounter issues:
   396→
   397→1. **NPM Authentication errors** → See [NPM_SETUP.md](./NPM_SETUP.md)
   398→2. Check this guide's troubleshooting section
   399→3. Review GitHub Actions logs
   400→4. Check NPM package status
   401→5. Contact @antoniopresto for repository/NPM access issues
   402→

content.content:
1→# Publish Workflow
     2→
     3→Execute this workflow to publish Belt packages to NPM.
     4→
     5→## Pre-publish Checklist
     6→
     7→1. Ensure all packages build successfully
     8→2. Run type checks
     9→3. Ensure @belt organization exists on npmjs.com
    10→4. Verify all tests pass
    11→
    12→## Quality Checks
    13→
    14→```bash
    15→# Build all packages
    16→bun run build
    17→
    18→# Type check
    19→bun run typecheck
    20→
    21→# Lint
    22→bun lint
    23→
    24→# Run tests
    25→bun test
    26→```
    27→
    28→## Publish Order
    29→
    30→Packages must be published in dependency order:
    31→
    32→| Order | Package | Dependencies |
    33→|-------|---------|--------------|
    34→| 1 | @belt/tools | None |
    35→| 2 | @belt/prettier-plugin | None |
    36→| 3 | @belt/compliance | None |
    37→| 4 | @belt/ai | @belt/tools |
    38→| 5 | @belt/cli | @belt/ai, @belt/tools |
    39→| 6 | belt-setup | @belt/ai, @belt/cli |
    40→
    41→## Publish Commands
    42→
    43→```bash
    44→# 1. Packages with no internal dependencies
    45→cd packages/tools && bun publish --access public
    46→cd packages/prettier-plugin && bun publish --access public
    47→cd packages/compliance && bun publish --access public
    48→
    49→# 2. Packages with dependencies (after above are published)
    50→cd packages/ai-tools && bun publish --access public
    51→cd packages/cli && bun publish --access public
    52→cd packages/belt-setup && bun publish --access public
    53→```
    54→
    55→Note: `--access public` is only required on first publish of each package.
    56→
    57→## Post-publish
    58→
    59→```bash
    60→# Create and push git tag
    61→git tag v<version>
    62→git push origin v<version>
    63→
    64→# Update harness progress
    65→# Edit .belt/harness/progress.txt to reflect published state
    66→```
    67→
    68→## Troubleshooting
    69→
    70→- **"Cannot resolve workspace protocol"**: Run from package root, not dist/
    71→- **"Access denied"**: Verify NPM login and org membership
    72→- **"Package not found"**: Ensure dependencies are published first (see order above)
    73→- **"E403 Forbidden"**: Check npm token permissions and package scope
    74→

1→# Quality Check
     2→
     3→Run all quality checks for the Belt monorepo.
     4→
     5→## Full Check
     6→
     7→```bash
     8→pnpm lint && pnpm build && pnpm typecheck
     9→```
    10→
    11→## Individual Checks
    12→
    13→```bash
    14→# Linting (Biome)
    15→pnpm lint
    16→
    17→# Fix auto-fixable issues
    18→pnpm lint:fix
    19→
    20→# Format code
    21→pnpm format
    22→
    23→# Type checking
    24→pnpm typecheck
    25→
    26→# Build all packages
    27→pnpm build
    28→
    29→# Run tests
    30→pnpm test
    31→
    32→# Clean build artifacts
    33→pnpm clean
    34→```
    35→
    36→## Pre-commit Workflow
    37→
    38→Git hooks automatically run:
    39→- `pre-commit`: pnpm lint
    40→- `pre-push`: pnpm build && pnpm typecheck
    41→- `commit-msg`: Validates conventional commits format
    42→

content.content:
1→# Changelog
     2→
     3→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
     4→
     5→The format is based on [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.1.0/),
     6→and this project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html).
     7→
     8→## [0.1.0] - 2024-12-29
     9→
    10→### Added
    11→
    12→- **@belt/core**: Plugin system with tapable hooks
    13→  - Lifecycle hooks: initialize, beforeCheck, checkResults, afterCheck
    14→  - Command hooks: registerCommands, beforeCommand, afterCommand
    15→  - Utility hooks: log, shutdown
    16→
    17→- **@belt/plugin-typecheck**: TypeScript diagnostic filtering
    18→  - Include/exclude patterns for file filtering
    19→  - Configurable tsconfig path
    20→  - Structured output with file locations
    21→
    22→- **@belt/plugin-sync-types**: Type declaration generation
    23→  - Discover imports from external projects
    24→  - Generate `declare module` statements
    25→  - Recursive type extraction with configurable depth
    26→
    27→- **@belt/cli**: Command-line interface
    28→  - React Ink TUI for interactive mode
    29→  - Commands: typecheck, sync-types, check, ui
    30→  - meow for argument parsing
    31→
    32→### Infrastructure
    33→
    34→- pnpm 9.14.2 + Turborepo 2.3.0 monorepo
    35→- Biome 2.3.8 for linting and formatting
    36→- TypeScript 5.9.3 with strict mode
    37→- tsup 8.3.0 for ESM builds
    38→- Husky 9.1.7 for git hooks
    39→

1→{
     2→  "name": "@belt/cli",
     3→  "description": "Belt CLI - Project scaffolding and AI harness for Claude Code",
     4→  "type": "module",
     5→  "sideEffects": false,
     6→  "version": "0.1.2",
     7→  "author": "Antonio Presto <antoniopresto@gmail.com>",
     8→  "license": "MIT",
     9→  "repository": {
    10→    "type": "git",
    11→    "url": "https://github.com/antoniopresto/belt",
    12→    "directory": "packages/cli"
    13→  },
    14→  "bugs": {
    15→    "url": "https://github.com/antoniopresto/belt/issues"
    16→  },
    17→  "homepage": "https://github.com/antoniopresto/belt/tree/main/packages/cli#readme",
    18→  "keywords": [
    19→    "cli",
    20→    "scaffolding",
    21→    "claude-code",
    22→    "harness",
    23→    "brainstorm",
    24→    "monorepo"
    25→  ],
    26→  "main": "./dist/index.cjs",
    27→  "module": "./dist/index.mjs",
    28→  "types": "./dist/index.d.ts",
    29→  "bin": {
    30→    "belt": "./dist/cli.mjs"
    31→  },
    32→  "exports": {
    33→    "./cli": {
    34→      "types": "./dist/cli.d.ts",
    35→      "import": "./dist/cli.mjs",
    36→      "require": "./dist/cli.cjs"
    37→    },
    38→    "./commands": {
    39→      "types": "./dist/commands.d.ts",
    40→      "import": "./dist/commands.mjs",
    41→      "require": "./dist/commands.cjs"
    42→    },
    43→    "./harness": {
    44→      "types": "./dist/harness.d.ts",
    45→      "import": "./dist/harness.mjs",
    46→      "require": "./dist/harness.cjs"
    47→    },
    48→    ".": {
    49→      "types": "./dist/index.d.ts",
    50→      "import": "./dist/index.mjs",
    51→      "require": "./dist/index.cjs"
    52→    },
    53→    "./templates": {
    54→      "types": "./dist/templates.d.ts",
    55→      "import": "./dist/templates.mjs",
    56→      "require": "./dist/templates.cjs"
    57→    },
    58→    "./utils": {
    59→      "types": "./dist/utils.d.ts",
    60→      "import": "./dist/utils.mjs",
    61→      "require": "./dist/utils.cjs"
    62→    }
    63→  },
    64→  "files": [
    65→    "dist"
    66→  ],
    67→  "scripts": {
    68→    "build": "vite build",
    69→    "dev": "vite build --watch",
    70→    "test": "vitest run --exclude '**/*.integration.test.ts' --exclude '**/*.e2e.test.ts' --passWithNoTests",
    71→    "test:watch": "vitest --watch --exclude '**/*.integration.test.ts' --exclude '**/*.e2e.test.ts'",
    72→    "test:integration": "vitest run --reporter=verbose src/__tests__/e2e",
    73→    "test:all": "vitest run",

content.content:
1→#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2→set -euo pipefail
     3→
     4→SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
     5→ROOT_DIR="$(dirname "$SCRIPT_DIR")"
     6→
     7→cd "$ROOT_DIR"
     8→
     9→current_version=$(jq -r '.version' package.json)
    10→
    11→if [[ ! "$current_version" =~ ^[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+$ ]]; then
    12→  echo "Error: Current version '$current_version' is not valid semver (X.Y.Z)"
    13→  exit 1
    14→fi
    15→
    16→echo "Current version: $current_version"
    17→
    18→IFS='.' read -r major minor patch <<< "$current_version"
    19→
    20→case "${1:-patch}" in
    21→  major)
    22→    new_version="$((major + 1)).0.0"
    23→    ;;
    24→  minor)
    25→    new_version="$major.$((minor + 1)).0"
    26→    ;;
    27→  patch)
    28→    new_version="$major.$minor.$((patch + 1))"
    29→    ;;
    30→  *)
    31→    echo "Usage: $0 [major|minor|patch]"
    32→    exit 1
    33→    ;;
    34→esac
    35→
    36→echo "New version: $new_version"
    37→
    38→if git rev-parse --verify "refs/tags/v$new_version" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    39→  echo "Error: Tag v$new_version already exists"
    40→  exit 1
    41→fi
    42→
    43→jq --arg v "$new_version" '.version = $v' package.json > package.json.tmp
    44→mv package.json.tmp package.json
    45→echo "Updated: package.json"
    46→
    47→for pkg_json in packages/*/package.json; do
    48→  jq --arg v "$new_version" '.version = $v' "$pkg_json" > "$pkg_json.tmp"
    49→  mv "$pkg_json.tmp" "$pkg_json"
    50→  echo "Updated: $pkg_json"
    51→done
    52→
    53→git add package.json packages/*/package.json
    54→
    55→git commit -m "chore: release v$new_version"
    56→
    57→git tag -a "v$new_version" -m "Release v$new_version"
    58→
    59→echo ""
    60→echo "Version bumped to $new_version"
    61→echo "Commit created"
    62→echo "Tag v$new_version created"
    63→echo ""
    64→echo "To publish, run:"
    65→echo "  git push origin main --tags"
    66→

1→# Belt
     2→
     3→TypeScript utility library providing runtime type validation, state management, and common helpers for mission-critical applications including WebGL games and high-frequency update scenarios (60fps+).
     4→
     5→## Packages
     6→
     7→| Package | Description |
     8→|---------|-------------|
     9→| [@belt/tools](./packages/tools) | TypeScript utilities (schema validation, state machine, helpers) |
    10→
    11→## Installation
    12→
    13→```bash
    14→npm install @belt/tools
    15→# or
    16→bun add @belt/tools
    17→```
    18→
    19→**Peer Dependencies:**
    20→- `react` >= 18.0.0 (optional, only for state-machine hooks)
    21→
    22→## Features
    23→
    24→- **Schema Validation** - Runtime type validation with TypeBox
    25→- **State Machine** - Immer-based state management with React hooks
    26→- **Helpers** - Common utilities (date, string, object, etc.)
    27→- **Type Utilities** - Advanced TypeScript type helpers
    28→- **Mission-Critical Ready** - Optimized for 60fps game loops
    29→
    30→## Quick Start
    31→
    32→### Schema Validation
    33→
    34→```tsx
    35→import { createType, createSchema, isType, InferType } from '@belt/tools';
    36→
    37→// Create a validated model
    38→const UserModel = createSchema({
    39→  id: 'number',
    40→  name: 'string',
    41→  email: 'string?',        // optional
    42→  roles: 'string[]',       // array
    43→  createdAt: 'date',       // Date object
    44→});
    45→
    46→type User = InferType<typeof UserModel>;
    47→
    48→// Parse and validate data
    49→const user = UserModel.parse({

content.content:
1→# Belt Project - Senior Research Report
     2→
     3→## Executive Summary
     4→
     5→This document consolidates research findings for preparing Belt monorepo for NPM publishing with Claude Code automation. The term "Anthropic Harness" refers to Anthropic's agent harness architecture documented in their engineering blog, specifically the patterns for building long-running AI agents that persist work across context windows.
     6→
     7→---
     8→
     9→## 1. Anthropic Harness Architecture
    10→
    11→### What It Is
    12→
    13→The "harness" is the orchestration layer that manages agent execution, context, and state persistence. Anthropic's Claude Agent SDK implements this pattern.
    14→
    15→### Core Problem Solved
    16→
    17→Long-running agents lose context between sessions. Each new context window starts blank. The harness provides mechanisms for agents to:
    18→- Resume work from previous sessions
    19→- Track progress across multiple sessions
    20→- Avoid duplicate work or premature completion claims
    21→
    22→### Two-Agent Pattern
    23→
    24→1. **Initializer Agent** (first session):
    25→   - Creates `init.sh` for environment setup
    26→   - Writes `claude-progress.txt` for human-readable state
    27→   - Generates comprehensive feature list (JSON format)
    28→   - Establishes git baseline
    29→
    30→2. **Coding Agent** (subsequent sessions):
    31→   - Follows startup ritual (pwd, read progress, review features)
    32→   - Implements one feature per session
    33→   - Updates progress file and commits changes
    34→   - Runs verification tests
    35→
    36→### Key Artifacts
    37→
    38→| Artifact | Purpose |
    39→|----------|---------|
    40→| `claude-progress.txt` | Human-readable progress summary |
    41→| `features.json` | Structured requirements (200+ items) |
    42→| `init.sh` | Environment initialization |
    43→| Git history | Technical change record |
    44→
    45→### Failure Patterns to Avoid
    46→
    47→1. **Premature completion** - Agent declares "done" without verification
    48→2. **Context exhaustion** - Trying too much in single session
    49→3. **Lost state** - Poor handoff documentation between sessions
    50→4. **Scope drift** - Agent removes tests or requirements

**Terminal Status**: `@belt/terminal` is a **separate standalone package** (not part of another package). It's an Astro-based web application for a custom Claude Code terminal interface with SQLite persistence.

file.content:
name: Publish

on:
  push:
    tags:
      - 'v*'

permissions:
  contents: write
  id-token: write

jobs:
  publish:
    name: Publish to NPM
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 15

    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Validate tag format
        run: |
          TAG="${GITHUB_REF#refs/tags/}"
          if [[ ! "$TAG" =~ ^v[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+$ ]]; then
            echo "::error::Invalid tag format: $TAG (expected vX.Y.Z)"
            exit 1
          fi
          echo "VERSION=${TAG#v}" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          echo "TAG=$TAG" >> $GITHUB_ENV

      - name: Verify version matches tag
        run: |
          PKG_VERSION=$(jq -r '.version' package.json)
          if [ "$PKG_VERSION" != "$VERSION" ]; then
            echo "::error::Tag $TAG doesn't match package.json version $PKG_VERSION"
            exit 1
          fi

      - name: Setup Bun
        uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2

      - name: Setup NPM authentication
        run: |
          echo "//registry.npmjs.org/:_authToken=${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}" > ~/.npmrc
          echo "registry=https://registry.npmjs.org/" >> ~/.npmrc

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: bun install --frozen-lockfile

      - name: Build all packages
        run: bun run build

      - name: Type check
        run: bun run check

      - name: Test
        run: bun run test

      - name: Verify dist files exist
        run: |
          for pkg in tools cli ai-tools belt-setup prettier-plugin; do
            if [ ! -d "packages/$pkg/dist" ]; then
              echo "::error::Missing dist for packages/$pkg"
              exit 1
            fi
          done

      - name: Publish @belt/tools
        working-directory: packages/tools
        env:
          NPM_CONFIG_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}
        run: bun publish --access public

      - name: Publish @belt/cli
        working-directory: packages/cli
        env:
          NPM_CONFIG_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}
        run: bun publish --access public

      - name: Publish @belt/ai
        working-directory: packages/ai-tools
        env:
          NPM_CONFIG_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}
        run: bun publish --access public

      - name: Publish belt-setup
        working-directory: packages/belt-setup
        env:
          NPM_CONFIG_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}
        run: bun publish --access public

      - name: Publish @belt/prettier-plugin
        working-directory: packages/prettier-plugin
        env:
          NPM_CONFIG_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}
        run: bun publish --access public

      - name: Create GitHub Release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
        with:
          tag_name: ${{ env.TAG }}
          name: ${{ env.TAG }}
          generate_release_notes: true

file.content:
# Publish Workflow

Execute this workflow to publish Belt packages to NPM.

## Pre-publish Checklist

1. Ensure all packages build successfully
2. Run type checks
3. Ensure @belt organization exists on npmjs.com
4. Verify all tests pass

## Quality Checks

```bash
# Build all packages
bun run build

# Type check
bun run typecheck

# Lint
bun lint

# Run tests
bun test
```

## Publish Order

Packages must be published in dependency order:

| Order | Package | Dependencies |
|-------|---------|--------------|
| 1 | @belt/tools | None |
| 2 | @belt/prettier-plugin | None |
| 3 | @belt/compliance | None |
| 4 | @belt/ai | @belt/tools |
| 5 | @belt/cli | @belt/ai, @belt/tools |
| 6 | belt-setup | @belt/ai, @belt/cli |

## Publish Commands

```bash
# 1. Packages with no internal dependencies
cd packages/tools && bun publish --access public
cd packages/prettier-plugin && bun publish --access public
cd packages/compliance && bun publish --access public

# 2. Packages with dependencies (after above are published)
cd packages/ai-tools && bun publish --access public
cd packages/cli && bun publish --access public
cd packages/belt-setup && bun publish --access public
```

Note: `--access public` is only required on first publish of each package.

## Post-publish

```bash
# Create and push git tag
git tag v<version>
git push origin v<version>

# Update harness progress
# Edit .belt/harness/progress.txt to reflect published state
```

## Troubleshooting

- **"Cannot resolve workspace protocol"**: Run from package root, not dist/
- **"Access denied"**: Verify NPM login and org membership
- **"Package not found"**: Ensure dependencies are published first (see order above)
- **"E403 Forbidden"**: Check npm token permissions and package scope

==================== !!! IMPORTANT !!! ==================
Test everything with end-to-end tests by accessing real
tools (Claude Code, Llama, etc.) in mock projects until
you obtain a complete and extremely reliable result.
Ultrathink! Use plan mode whenever you need to.
=========================================================

## Current State

- **Status**: Active development - 6 packages

| belt-setup | 0.1.2 | Setup utility | Published |

| @belt/compliance | 0.1.2 | Activity monitor | Published |

3. Review `features.json` for pending tasks

## Next Steps

# Belt Monorepo

TypeScript utility library with AI integration, CLI tools, and developer utilities.

## IMPORTANT (ALL RULES MUST BE FOLLOWED):
- THINK, PLAN, AND EXECUTE/WRITE CODE IN ENGLISH, ANSWER CHAT USING PORTUGUESE.
-  **ALL (EVEN SMALL) TASKS MUST BE DELIVERED PRODUCTION-READY!**:
  - ALL (EVEN SMALL) TASKS MUST BE DELIVERED PRODUCTION-READY - **ALL (EVEN SMALL) TASKS MUST BE DELIVERED PRODUCTION-READY!**
  - NO MOCKS IN CODE ARE ALLOWED - **ALL (EVEN SMALL) TASKS MUST BE DELIVERED PRODUCTION-READY!**
  - AGAIN: **ALL (EVEN SMALL) TASKS MUST BE DELIVERED PRODUCTION-READY!**
  - FULL TEST IS MANDATORY - **ALL (EVEN SMALL) TASKS MUST BE DELIVERED PRODUCTION-READY!**:
    - MANUAL, INTEGRATION AND UNIT TESTS ARE REQUIRED FOR EVERY TASK -  **ALL (EVEN SMALL) TASKS MUST BE DELIVERED PRODUCTION-READY!**

## Project Structure

```
belt/
├── .belt/
│   └── harness/         # Progress tracking and features
├── .claude/
│   ├── settings.json    # Claude Code hooks configuration
│   └── commands/        # Skill workflows (publish, quality)
└── packages/
    ├── tools/           # @belt/tools - TypeScript utilities (schema, state-machine, helpers)
    ├── ai-tools/        # @belt/ai - Claude Code integration (AgentRC, hooks)
    ├── cli/             # @belt/cli - CLI with harness system
    ├── belt-setup/      # belt-setup - Setup utility
    ├── prettier-plugin/ # @belt/prettier-plugin - Line-break formatting
    └── compliance/      # @belt/compliance - Activity monitor
```

## Tech Stack

- **Monorepo**: Bun + Turborepo 2.3.0
- **Build**: tsup 8.3.0
- **Linting/Formatting**: Biome 2.3.8
- **TypeScript**: 5.9.3 (strict mode)
- **Testing**: Vitest

## Packages

| Package | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| @belt/tools | Schema validation, state machine, helpers, type utilities |
| @belt/ai | Claude Code integration, AgentRC rules, hook runner |
| @belt/cli | CLI commands, harness system (agents, brainstorm, router) |
| belt-setup | Setup utility for initializing projects |
| @belt/prettier-plugin | Prettier plugin for line-break formatting |
| @belt/compliance | Activity monitor daemon |

## Development

```bash
# Install dependencies
bun install

# Build
bun run build

# Run tests
bun test

# Type check
bun run typecheck

# Lint
bun lint
```

## NPM Publishing

```bash
# Build and verify
bun run build && bun run typecheck

# Publish order (dependency order):
# 1. @belt/tools (no internal deps)
# 2. @belt/prettier-plugin (no internal deps)
# 3. @belt/compliance (no internal deps)
# 4. @belt/ai (depends on tools)
# 5. @belt/cli (depends on ai, tools)
# 6. belt-setup (depends on ai, cli)

cd packages/tools && bun publish --access public
cd packages/prettier-plugin && bun publish --access public
cd packages/compliance && bun publish --access public
cd packages/ai-tools && bun publish --access public
cd packages/cli && bun publish --access public
cd packages/belt-setup && bun publish --access public
```

## Configuration

- **GitHub**: antoniopresto
- **Project directory**: `~/antonio/belt`

---

## Claude Code Automation

### Git Hooks (Husky)

- `pre-commit`: Runs `bun lint`
- `pre-push`: Runs `bun run build && bun run typecheck`
- `commit-msg`: Validates conventional commits format

### Incremental Commits Policy

Commit format:
```
type(scope): description
```

Types: feat|fix|docs|style|refactor|perf|test|build|ci|chore|revert

---

## User Preferences (for Claude)

- Write code and docs in English
- Use TypeScript with strict types
- Use Biome for linting/formatting
- No "AI" comments in code
- Be pragmatic, not bureaucratic
- Commits: incremental, no AI attribution


==================== !!! IMPORTANT !!! ==================
Test everything with end-to-end tests by accessing real
tools (Claude Code, Llama, etc.) in mock projects until
you obtain a complete and extremely reliable result.
Ultrathink! Use plan mode whenever you need to.
=========================================================

content.content:
1→# Publishing Guide
     2→
     3→This guide explains how to publish new versions of Belt packages to NPM.
     4→
     5→## Quick Start
     6→
     7→```bash
     8→# 1. Ensure you're on main branch with clean working tree
     9→git checkout main
    10→git status  # Should be clean
    11→
    12→# 2. Bump version (choose one)
    13→bun run version:patch  # 0.0.987 → 0.0.988 (bug fixes)
    14→bun run version:minor  # 0.0.987 → 0.1.0   (new features)
    15→bun run version:major  # 0.0.987 → 1.0.0   (breaking changes)
    16→
    17→# 3. Push commit and tags to trigger automated publishing
    18→git push origin main --tags
    19→```
    20→
    21→That's it! GitHub Actions will automatically:
    22→- Build all packages
    23→- Run tests and type checks
    24→- Publish to NPM
    25→- Create a GitHub Release
    26→
    27→## Semantic Versioning
    28→
    29→We follow [Semantic Versioning 2.0.0](https://semver.org/):
    30→
    31→| Version Type | When to Use | Example |
    32→|--------------|-------------|---------|
    33→| **Patch** (`x.y.Z`) | Bug fixes, documentation, internal changes | `0.0.987 → 0.0.988` |
    34→| **Minor** (`x.Y.0`) | New features (backward compatible) | `0.0.987 → 0.1.0` |
    35→| **Major** (`X.0.0`) | Breaking changes | `0.0.987 → 1.0.0` |
    36→
    37→## The `version:*` Scripts
    38→
    39→Located in `scripts/bump-version.sh`, these scripts:
    40→
    41→### What They Do
    42→
    43→1. ✅ Validate current version is valid semver (X.Y.Z)
    44→2. ✅ Calculate new version based on bump type
    45→3. ✅ Check that tag `vX.Y.Z` doesn't already exist
    46→4. ✅ Update version in root `package.json`
    47→5. ✅ Update version in ALL package `package.json` files:
    48→   - `packages/tools/package.json`
    49→   - `packages/cli/package.json`
    50→   - `packages/ai-tools/package.json`
    51→   - `packages/belt-setup/package.json`
    52→   - `packages/prettier-plugin/package.json`
    53→6. ✅ Create git commit: `chore: release vX.Y.Z`
    54→7. ✅ Create git tag: `vX.Y.Z`
    55→
    56→### What They DON'T Do
    57→
    58→- ❌ Push to remote (you must do this manually)
    59→- ❌ Publish to NPM (GitHub Actions does this)
    60→- ❌ Create GitHub Release (GitHub Actions does this)
    61→
    62→## GitHub Actions Publish Workflow
    63→
    64→When you push a tag matching `v*` pattern, the `.github/workflows/publish.yml` workflow runs:
    65→
    66→### Workflow Steps
    67→
    68→```mermaid
    69→graph TD
    70→    A[Push tag v0.0.988] --> B[Validate tag format]
    71→    B --> C[Verify version matches package.json]
    72→    C --> D[Setup Bun]
    73→    D --> E[Install dependencies]
    74→    E --> F[Build all packages]
    75→    F --> G[Type check]
    76→    G --> H[Run tests]
    77→    H --> I[Verify dist/ exists]
    78→    I --> J[Publish @belt/tools with bun]
    79→    J --> K[Publish @belt/cli with bun]
    80→    K --> L[Publish @belt/ai with bun]
    81→    L --> M[Publish belt-setup with bun]
    82→    M --> N[Publish @belt/prettier-plugin with bun]
    83→    N --> O[Create GitHub Release]
    84→```
    85→
    86→**Note:** We use `bun publish` instead of `npm publish` to automatically resolve `workspace:*` dependencies to actual version numbers.
    87→
    88→### Packages Published
    89→
    90→The workflow publishes these packages to NPM:
    91→
    92→| Package | NPM Name | Access |
    93→|---------|----------|--------|
    94→| tools | `@belt/tools` | public |
    95→| cli | `@belt/cli` | public |
    96→| ai-tools | `@belt/ai` | public |
    97→| belt-setup | `belt-setup` | public |
    98→| prettier-plugin | `@belt/prettier-plugin` | public |
    99→
   100→### Authentication
   101→
   102→The workflow requires an **NPM Access Token** configured as a GitHub Secret:
   103→
   104→**⚠️ CRITICAL: NPM Token Setup Required**
   105→
   106→Before publishing, you must configure the NPM token:
   107→
   108→1. **Create NPM Granular Access Token** at https://www.npmjs.com/settings/[username]/tokens
   109→2. **Add to GitHub Secrets** as `NPM_TOKEN` at https://github.com/antoniopresto/belt/settings/secrets/actions
   110→
   111→See **[NPM_SETUP.md](./NPM_SETUP.md)** for complete setup instructions.
   112→
   113→**Token Details:**
   114→- Type: Granular Access Token (not classic)
   115→- Expiration: 90 days maximum
   116→- Permissions: Read and write on all `@belt/*` packages
   117→- 2FA: Required by default
   118→
   119→## Complete Publishing Example
   120→
   121→### Step-by-Step Walkthrough
   122→
   123→```bash
   124→# Check current status
   125→cd /Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/belt
   126→git status
   127→# On branch main
   128→# Your branch is up to date with 'origin/main'.
   129→# nothing to commit, working tree clean
   130→
   131→# Check current version
   132→cat package.json | grep version
   133→#   "version": "0.0.987",
   134→
   135→# Bump patch version
   136→bun run version:patch
   137→
   138→# Output:
   139→# Current version: 0.0.987
   140→# New version: 0.0.988
   141→# Updated: package.json
   142→# Updated: packages/ai-tools/package.json
   143→# Updated: packages/belt-setup/package.json
   144→# Updated: packages/cli/package.json
   145→# Updated: packages/prettier-plugin/package.json
   146→# Updated: packages/tools/package.json
   147→# [main abc1234] chore: release v0.0.988
   148→#  6 files changed, 6 insertions(+), 6 deletions(-)
   149→#
   150→# Version bumped to 0.0.988
   151→# Commit created
   152→# Tag v0.0.988 created
   153→#
   154→# To publish, run:
   155→#   git push origin main --tags
   156→
   157→# Verify the commit
   158→git log -1
   159→# commit abc1234...
   160→# Author: Your Name <your.email@example.com>
   161→# Date:   Fri Jan 10 12:34:56 2026 -0300
   162→#
   163→#     chore: release v0.0.988
   164→
   165→# Verify the tag
   166→git tag -l | tail -1
   167→# v0.0.988
   168→
   169→# Push commit and tags
   170→git push origin main --tags
   171→# Enumerating objects: 15, done.
   172→# Counting objects: 100% (15/15), done.
   173→# ...
   174→# To github.com:antoniopresto/belt.git
   175→#    abc1233..abc1234  main -> main
   176→#  * [new tag]         v0.0.988 -> v0.0.988
   177→```
   178→
   179→### Monitor the Workflow
   180→
   181→1. Go to GitHub Actions: https://github.com/antoniopresto/belt/actions
   182→2. You should see a "Publish" workflow running
   183→3. Wait for it to complete (~2-5 minutes)
   184→
   185→### Verify Publication
   186→
   187→```bash
   188→# Check NPM versions (wait ~1 minute after workflow completes)
   189→npm view @belt/tools version
   190→npm view @belt/cli version
   191→npm view @belt/ai version
   192→npm view belt-setup version
   193→npm view @belt/prettier-plugin version
   194→
   195→# All should return: 0.0.988
   196→```
   197→
   198→### Check GitHub Release
   199→
   200→1. Go to: https://github.com/antoniopresto/belt/releases
   201→2. You should see a new release: `v0.0.988`
   202→3. Release notes are auto-generated from commits
   203→
   204→## Troubleshooting
   205→
   206→### Tag Already Exists
   207→
   208→**Error:**
   209→```
   210→Error: Tag v0.0.988 already exists
   211→```
   212→
   213→**Solution:**
   214→```bash
   215→# Delete local tag
   216→git tag -d v0.0.988
   217→
   218→# Delete remote tag (if pushed)
   219→git push origin :refs/tags/v0.0.988
   220→
   221→# Try again
   222→bun run version:patch
   223→```
   224→
   225→### Version Mismatch
   226→
   227→**Error in GitHub Actions:**
   228→```
   229→Error: Tag v0.0.988 doesn't match package.json version 0.0.987
   230→```
   231→
   232→**Solution:**
   233→This should never happen if you use the `version:*` scripts. If it does:
   234→
   235→```bash
   236→# Manually fix all package.json files
   237→# Then:
   238→git add package.json packages/*/package.json
   239→git commit --amend --no-edit
   240→git tag -f v0.0.988
   241→git push origin main --tags --force
   242→```
   243→
   244→### NPM Publish Permission Denied
   245→
   246→**Error in GitHub Actions:**
   247→```
   248→npm ERR! 403 Forbidden - PUT https://registry.npmjs.org/@belt/tools
   249→```
   250→
   251→**Solution:**
   252→- Ensure you're a collaborator/owner on NPM organization `@belt`
   253→- Verify NPM OIDC is configured correctly
   254→- Contact repository owner for access
   255→
   256→### Workflow Not Triggered
   257→
   258→**Problem:** Pushed tags but workflow didn't run
   259→
   260→**Check:**
   261→```bash
   262→# Ensure tag matches pattern v*
   263→git tag -l | grep v0.0.988
   264→# v0.0.988
   265→
   266→# Ensure tag was pushed to remote
   267→git ls-remote --tags origin | grep v0.0.988
   268→# abc1234... refs/tags/v0.0.988
   269→```
   270→
   271→### Build/Test Failures
   272→
   273→**Problem:** Workflow fails during build or test
   274→
   275→**Solution:**
   276→1. Run locally first:
   277→   ```bash
   278→   bun run build
   279→   bun run check
   280→   bun run test
   281→   ```
   282→2. Fix any errors
   283→3. Commit fixes
   284→4. Delete failed tag and retry:
   285→   ```bash
   286→   git tag -d v0.0.988
   287→   git push origin :refs/tags/v0.0.988
   288→   bun run version:patch
   289→   git push origin main --tags
   290→   ```
   291→
   292→## Pre-Release Checklist
   293→
   294→Before publishing, ensure:
   295→
   296→- [ ] **NPM Token is configured** in GitHub Secrets (see [NPM_SETUP.md](./NPM_SETUP.md))
   297→- [ ] All tests pass locally: `bun run test`
   298→- [ ] Build succeeds: `bun run build`
   299→- [ ] Type check passes: `bun run check`
   300→- [ ] Linting passes: `bun lint`
   301→- [ ] All changes are committed
   302→- [ ] You're on the `main` branch
   303→- [ ] Working tree is clean: `git status`
   304→- [ ] You've pulled latest changes: `git pull`
   305→- [ ] CHANGELOG.md is updated (optional but recommended)
   306→
   307→## Rolling Back a Release
   308→
   309→If you need to unpublish or rollback:
   310→
   311→### Unpublish from NPM (within 72 hours)
   312→
   313→```bash
   314→# Unpublish specific version
   315→npm unpublish @belt/tools@0.0.988
   316→npm unpublish @belt/cli@0.0.988
   317→npm unpublish @belt/ai@0.0.988
   318→npm unpublish belt-setup@0.0.988
   319→npm unpublish @belt/prettier-plugin@0.0.988
   320→```
   321→
   322→### Delete GitHub Release
   323→
   324→1. Go to: https://github.com/antoniopresto/belt/releases
   325→2. Find the release
   326→3. Click "Delete"
   327→
   328→### Delete Git Tag
   329→
   330→```bash
   331→# Delete local tag
   332→git tag -d v0.0.988
   333→
   334→# Delete remote tag
   335→git push origin :refs/tags/v0.0.988
   336→```
   337→
   338→### Revert Version Commit
   339→
   340→```bash
   341→# Revert the release commit
   342→git revert HEAD
   343→git push origin main
   344→```
   345→
   346→## Manual Publishing (Emergency Only)
   347→
   348→If GitHub Actions is down, you can publish manually:
   349→
   350→```bash
   351→# Build all packages
   352→bun run build
   353→
   354→# Verify everything works
   355→bun run check
   356→bun run test
   357→
   358→# Login to NPM (if not already logged in)
   359→npm login
   360→
   361→# Publish each package manually (using bun to resolve workspace:* dependencies)
   362→cd packages/tools && bun publish --access public
   363→cd ../cli && bun publish --access public
   364→cd ../ai-tools && bun publish --access public
   365→cd ../belt-setup && bun publish --access public
   366→cd ../prettier-plugin && bun publish --access public
   367→```
   368→
   369→**⚠️ Warning:** Manual publishing bypasses automated checks and should only be used in emergencies.
   370→
   371→**Why use `bun publish` instead of `npm publish`?**
   372→- Bun automatically resolves `workspace:*` dependencies to actual version numbers
   373→- npm would publish with `workspace:*` which breaks installation for end users
   374→
   375→## Best Practices
   376→
   377→1. **Always use the `version:*` scripts** - Never manually edit version numbers
   378→2. **Test before releasing** - Run full test suite locally
   379→3. **Update CHANGELOG.md** - Document changes for users
   380→4. **Use conventional commits** - Makes it easier to generate changelogs
   381→5. **Release often** - Small, frequent releases are better than large ones
   382→6. **Monitor the workflow** - Watch GitHub Actions to catch issues early
   383→7. **Verify publication** - Check NPM after workflow completes
   384→
   385→## Related Documentation
   386→
   387→- **[NPM_SETUP.md](./NPM_SETUP.md)** - NPM token setup and troubleshooting (READ THIS FIRST!)
   388→- [TESTING.md](./TESTING.md) - How to run tests
   389→- [README.md](./README.md) - Project overview and development
   390→- [CHANGELOG.md](./CHANGELOG.md) - Version history
   391→- [GitHub Actions Workflows](./.github/workflows/) - CI/CD configuration
   392→
   393→## Support
   394→
   395→If you encounter issues:
   396→
   397→1. **NPM Authentication errors** → See [NPM_SETUP.md](./NPM_SETUP.md)
   398→2. Check this guide's troubleshooting section
   399→3. Review GitHub Actions logs
   400→4. Check NPM package status
   401→5. Contact @antoniopresto for repository/NPM access issues
   402→

# Publishing Guide

This guide explains how to publish new versions of Belt packages to NPM.

## Quick Start

```bash
# 1. Ensure you're on main branch with clean working tree
git checkout main
git status  # Should be clean

# 2. Bump version (choose one)
bun run version:patch  # 0.0.987 → 0.0.988 (bug fixes)
bun run version:minor  # 0.0.987 → 0.1.0   (new features)
bun run version:major  # 0.0.987 → 1.0.0   (breaking changes)

# 3. Push commit and tags to trigger automated publishing
git push origin main --tags
```

That's it! GitHub Actions will automatically:
- Build all packages
- Run tests and type checks
- Publish to NPM
- Create a GitHub Release

## Semantic Versioning

We follow [Semantic Versioning 2.0.0](https://semver.org/):

| Version Type | When to Use | Example |
|--------------|-------------|---------|
| **Patch** (`x.y.Z`) | Bug fixes, documentation, internal changes | `0.0.987 → 0.0.988` |
| **Minor** (`x.Y.0`) | New features (backward compatible) | `0.0.987 → 0.1.0` |
| **Major** (`X.0.0`) | Breaking changes | `0.0.987 → 1.0.0` |

## The `version:*` Scripts

Located in `scripts/bump-version.sh`, these scripts:

### What They Do

1. ✅ Validate current version is valid semver (X.Y.Z)
2. ✅ Calculate new version based on bump type
3. ✅ Check that tag `vX.Y.Z` doesn't already exist
4. ✅ Update version in root `package.json`
5. ✅ Update version in ALL package `package.json` files:
   - `packages/tools/package.json`
   - `packages/cli/package.json`
   - `packages/ai-tools/package.json`
   - `packages/belt-setup/package.json`
   - `packages/prettier-plugin/package.json`
6. ✅ Create git commit: `chore: release vX.Y.Z`
7. ✅ Create git tag: `vX.Y.Z`

### What They DON'T Do

- ❌ Push to remote (you must do this manually)
- ❌ Publish to NPM (GitHub Actions does this)
- ❌ Create GitHub Release (GitHub Actions does this)

## GitHub Actions Publish Workflow

When you push a tag matching `v*` pattern, the `.github/workflows/publish.yml` workflow runs:

### Workflow Steps

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Push tag v0.0.988] --> B[Validate tag format]
    B --> C[Verify version matches package.json]
    C --> D[Setup Bun]
    D --> E[Install dependencies]
    E --> F[Build all packages]
    F --> G[Type check]
    G --> H[Run tests]
    H --> I[Verify dist/ exists]
    I --> J[Publish @belt/tools with bun]
    J --> K[Publish @belt/cli with bun]
    K --> L[Publish @belt/ai with bun]
    L --> M[Publish belt-setup with bun]
    M --> N[Publish @belt/prettier-plugin with bun]
    N --> O[Create GitHub Release]
```

**Note:** We use `bun publish` instead of `npm publish` to automatically resolve `workspace:*` dependencies to actual version numbers.

### Packages Published

The workflow publishes these packages to NPM:

| Package | NPM Name | Access |
|---------|----------|--------|
| tools | `@belt/tools` | public |
| cli | `@belt/cli` | public |
| ai-tools | `@belt/ai` | public |
| belt-setup | `belt-setup` | public |
| prettier-plugin | `@belt/prettier-plugin` | public |

### Authentication

The workflow requires an **NPM Access Token** configured as a GitHub Secret:

**⚠️ CRITICAL: NPM Token Setup Required**

Before publishing, you must configure the NPM token:

1. **Create NPM Granular Access Token** at https://www.npmjs.com/settings/[username]/tokens
2. **Add to GitHub Secrets** as `NPM_TOKEN` at https://github.com/antoniopresto/belt/settings/secrets/actions

See **[NPM_SETUP.md](./NPM_SETUP.md)** for complete setup instructions.

**Token Details:**
- Type: Granular Access Token (not classic)
- Expiration: 90 days maximum
- Permissions: Read and write on all `@belt/*` packages
- 2FA: Required by default

## Complete Publishing Example

### Step-by-Step Walkthrough

```bash
# Check current status
cd /Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/belt
git status
# On branch main
# Your branch is up to date with 'origin/main'.
# nothing to commit, working tree clean

# Check current version
cat package.json | grep version
#   "version": "0.0.987",

# Bump patch version
bun run version:patch

# Output:
# Current version: 0.0.987
# New version: 0.0.988
# Updated: package.json
# Updated: packages/ai-tools/package.json
# Updated: packages/belt-setup/package.json
# Updated: packages/cli/package.json
# Updated: packages/prettier-plugin/package.json
# Updated: packages/tools/package.json
# [main abc1234] chore: release v0.0.988
#  6 files changed, 6 insertions(+), 6 deletions(-)
#
# Version bumped to 0.0.988
# Commit created
# Tag v0.0.988 created
#
# To publish, run:
#   git push origin main --tags

# Verify the commit
git log -1
# commit abc1234...
# Author: Your Name <your.email@example.com>
# Date:   Fri Jan 10 12:34:56 2026 -0300
#
#     chore: release v0.0.988

# Verify the tag
git tag -l | tail -1
# v0.0.988

# Push commit and tags
git push origin main --tags
# Enumerating objects: 15, done.
# Counting objects: 100% (15/15), done.
# ...
# To github.com:antoniopresto/belt.git
#    abc1233..abc1234  main -> main
#  * [new tag]         v0.0.988 -> v0.0.988
```

### Monitor the Workflow

1. Go to GitHub Actions: https://github.com/antoniopresto/belt/actions
2. You should see a "Publish" workflow running
3. Wait for it to complete (~2-5 minutes)

### Verify Publication

```bash
# Check NPM versions (wait ~1 minute after workflow completes)
npm view @belt/tools version
npm view @belt/cli version
npm view @belt/ai version
npm view belt-setup version
npm view @belt/prettier-plugin version

# All should return: 0.0.988
```

### Check GitHub Release

1. Go to: https://github.com/antoniopresto/belt/releases
2. You should see a new release: `v0.0.988`
3. Release notes are auto-generated from commits

## Troubleshooting

### Tag Already Exists

**Error:**
```
Error: Tag v0.0.988 already exists
```

**Solution:**
```bash
# Delete local tag
git tag -d v0.0.988

# Delete remote tag (if pushed)
git push origin :refs/tags/v0.0.988

# Try again
bun run version:patch
```

### Version Mismatch

**Error in GitHub Actions:**
```
Error: Tag v0.0.988 doesn't match package.json version 0.0.987
```

**Solution:**
This should never happen if you use the `version:*` scripts. If it does:

```bash
# Manually fix all package.json files
# Then:
git add package.json packages/*/package.json
git commit --amend --no-edit
git tag -f v0.0.988
git push origin main --tags --force
```

### NPM Publish Permission Denied

**Error in GitHub Actions:**
```
npm ERR! 403 Forbidden - PUT https://registry.npmjs.org/@belt/tools
```

**Solution:**
- Ensure you're a collaborator/owner on NPM organization `@belt`
- Verify NPM OIDC is configured correctly
- Contact repository owner for access

### Workflow Not Triggered

**Problem:** Pushed tags but workflow didn't run

**Check:**
```bash
# Ensure tag matches pattern v*
git tag -l | grep v0.0.988
# v0.0.988

# Ensure tag was pushed to remote
git ls-remote --tags origin | grep v0.0.988
# abc1234... refs/tags/v0.0.988
```

### Build/Test Failures

**Problem:** Workflow fails during build or test

**Solution:**
1. Run locally first:
   ```bash
   bun run build
   bun run check
   bun run test
   ```
2. Fix any errors
3. Commit fixes
4. Delete failed tag and retry:
   ```bash
   git tag -d v0.0.988
   git push origin :refs/tags/v0.0.988
   bun run version:patch
   git push origin main --tags
   ```

## Pre-Release Checklist

Before publishing, ensure:

- [ ] **NPM Token is configured** in GitHub Secrets (see [NPM_SETUP.md](./NPM_SETUP.md))
- [ ] All tests pass locally: `bun run test`
- [ ] Build succeeds: `bun run build`
- [ ] Type check passes: `bun run check`
- [ ] Linting passes: `bun lint`
- [ ] All changes are committed
- [ ] You're on the `main` branch
- [ ] Working tree is clean: `git status`
- [ ] You've pulled latest changes: `git pull`
- [ ] CHANGELOG.md is updated (optional but recommended)

## Rolling Back a Release

If you need to unpublish or rollback:

### Unpublish from NPM (within 72 hours)

```bash
# Unpublish specific version
npm unpublish @belt/tools@0.0.988
npm unpublish @belt/cli@0.0.988
npm unpublish @belt/ai@0.0.988
npm unpublish belt-setup@0.0.988
npm unpublish @belt/prettier-plugin@0.0.988
```

### Delete GitHub Release

1. Go to: https://github.com/antoniopresto/belt/releases
2. Find the release
3. Click "Delete"

### Delete Git Tag

```bash
# Delete local tag
git tag -d v0.0.988

# Delete remote tag
git push origin :refs/tags/v0.0.988
```

### Revert Version Commit

```bash
# Revert the release commit
git revert HEAD
git push origin main
```

## Manual Publishing (Emergency Only)

If GitHub Actions is down, you can publish manually:

```bash
# Build all packages
bun run build

# Verify everything works
bun run check
bun run test

# Login to NPM (if not already logged in)
npm login

# Publish each package manually (using bun to resolve workspace:* dependencies)
cd packages/tools && bun publish --access public
cd ../cli && bun publish --access public
cd ../ai-tools && bun publish --access public
cd ../belt-setup && bun publish --access public
cd ../prettier-plugin && bun publish --access public
```

**⚠️ Warning:** Manual publishing bypasses automated checks and should only be used in emergencies.

**Why use `bun publish` instead of `npm publish`?**
- Bun automatically resolves `workspace:*` dependencies to actual version numbers
- npm would publish with `workspace:*` which breaks installation for end users

## Best Practices

1. **Always use the `version:*` scripts** - Never manually edit version numbers
2. **Test before releasing** - Run full test suite locally
3. **Update CHANGELOG.md** - Document changes for users
4. **Use conventional commits** - Makes it easier to generate changelogs
5. **Release often** - Small, frequent releases are better than large ones
6. **Monitor the workflow** - Watch GitHub Actions to catch issues early
7. **Verify publication** - Check NPM after workflow completes

## Related Documentation

- **[NPM_SETUP.md](./NPM_SETUP.md)** - NPM token setup and troubleshooting (READ THIS FIRST!)
- [TESTING.md](./TESTING.md) - How to run tests
- [README.md](./README.md) - Project overview and development
- [CHANGELOG.md](./CHANGELOG.md) - Version history
- [GitHub Actions Workflows](./.github/workflows/) - CI/CD configuration

## Support

If you encounter issues:

1. **NPM Authentication errors** → See [NPM_SETUP.md](./NPM_SETUP.md)
2. Check this guide's troubleshooting section
3. Review GitHub Actions logs
4. Check NPM package status
5. Contact @antoniopresto for repository/NPM access issues

file.content:
# Changelog

All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.

The format is based on [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.1.0/),
and this project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html).

## [0.1.0] - 2024-12-29

### Added

- **@belt/core**: Plugin system with tapable hooks
  - Lifecycle hooks: initialize, beforeCheck, checkResults, afterCheck
  - Command hooks: registerCommands, beforeCommand, afterCommand
  - Utility hooks: log, shutdown

- **@belt/plugin-typecheck**: TypeScript diagnostic filtering
  - Include/exclude patterns for file filtering
  - Configurable tsconfig path
  - Structured output with file locations

- **@belt/plugin-sync-types**: Type declaration generation
  - Discover imports from external projects
  - Generate `declare module` statements
  - Recursive type extraction with configurable depth

- **@belt/cli**: Command-line interface
  - React Ink TUI for interactive mode
  - Commands: typecheck, sync-types, check, ui
  - meow for argument parsing

### Infrastructure

- pnpm 9.14.2 + Turborepo 2.3.0 monorepo
- Biome 2.3.8 for linting and formatting
- TypeScript 5.9.3 with strict mode
- tsup 8.3.0 for ESM builds
- Husky 9.1.7 for git hooks

content.content:
1→# @belt/compliance
     2→
     3→Keep MS Teams status always "Available" through imperceptible mouse movements that reset the system inactivity timer.
     4→
     5→## Why This Works
     6→
     7→Teams uses the macOS `HIDIdleTime` API to detect inactivity. This API is a kernel timer that resets on any HID (mouse/keyboard) event. By simulating imperceptible mouse movements (1-2 pixels), the timer stays at zero.
     8→
     9→## Installation
    10→
    11→### Global Installation (Recommended)
    12→
    13→```bash
    14→npm install -g @belt/compliance
    15→# or
    16→bun add -g @belt/compliance
    17→```
    18→
    19→### Local Development
    20→
    21→```bash
    22→cd packages/compliance
    23→bun install
    24→bun run build
    25→```
    26→
    27→## Permission Configuration (REQUIRED)
    28→
    29→The app requires **Accessibility permission** to simulate mouse events:
    30→
    31→1. Open **System Settings** → **Privacy & Security** → **Accessibility**
    32→2. Click the lock icon to unlock
    33→3. Click **+** and add:
    34→   - **Terminal** (if running via terminal)
    35→   - **iTerm** (if using iTerm)
    36→   - **Visual Studio Code** (if running via VS Code)
    37→   - Or the specific app that will execute the script
    38→
    39→## CLI Commands
    40→
    41→### Start the Daemon
    42→
    43→```bash
    44→# Start in foreground (verbose)
    45→compliance start -v
    46→
    47→# Start as background daemon
    48→compliance start --daemon
    49→
    50→# Start with custom options
    51→compliance start -i 30000 -p 5 -t 10000 -m both -v
    52→```
    53→
    54→### Stop the Daemon
    55→
    56→```bash
    57→compliance stop
    58→```
    59→
    60→### Check Status
    61→
    62→```bash
    63→compliance status
    64→```
    65→
    66→Output example:
    67→```
    68→Status: ✅ Running (PID: 12345)
    69→
    70→┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
    71→│            Statistics               │
    72→├─────────────────────────────────────┤
    73→│  Uptime          │ 2h 34m 12s       │
    74→│  Total jiggles   │ 156              │
    75→│  Mouse jiggles   │ 142              │
    76→│  Keyboard jiggles│ 14               │
    77→│  Skipped (active)│ 23               │
    78→│  Last jiggle     │ 2s ago           │
    79→└─────────────────────────────────────┘
    80→```
    81→
    82→### Manage Configuration
    83→
    84→```bash
    85→# Show all config
    86→compliance config
    87→
    88→# Set a value
    89→compliance config set intervalMs 30000
    90→compliance config set mode both
    91→compliance config set verbose true
    92→
    93→# Get a value
    94→compliance config get intervalMs
    95→
    96→# Reset to defaults
    97→compliance config reset
    98→```
    99→
   100→### View Logs
   101→
   102→```bash
   103→# Show last 50 lines
   104→compliance logs
   105→
   106→# Show last N lines
   107→compliance logs -n 100
   108→
   109→# Follow logs (like tail -f)
   110→compliance logs -f
   111→```
   112→
   113→### Autostart on Login (macOS)
   114→
   115→```bash
   116→# Show autostart status
   117→compliance autostart
   118→
   119→# Enable autostart on login
   120→compliance autostart enable
   121→
   122→# Disable autostart
   123→compliance autostart disable
   124→```
   125→
   126→When enabled, compliance will start automatically when you log in to your Mac. It uses macOS LaunchAgent at `~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.user.compliance.plist`.
   127→
   128→## Configuration Options
   129→
   130→| Key | Description | Default | Range |
   131→|-----|-------------|---------|-------|
   132→| `intervalMs` | Interval between activities (ms) | 60000 | >= 1000 |
   133→| `movementPx` | Mouse movement distance (pixels) | 2 | 1-50 |
   134→| `idleThresholdMs` | Idle time before activating (ms) | 30000 | >= 5000 |
   135→| `mode` | Activity mode | mouse | mouse, keyboard, both |
   136→| `verbose` | Enable verbose logging | false | true, false |
   137→
   138→## CLI Options for `start`
   139→
   140→| Flag | Description |
   141→|------|-------------|
   142→| `-i, --interval <ms>` | Override interval |
   143→| `-p, --pixels <px>` | Override pixel distance |
   144→| `-t, --threshold <ms>` | Override idle threshold |
   145→| `-m, --mode <mode>` | Override mode |
   146→| `-v, --verbose` | Enable verbose logging |
   147→| `-d, --daemon` | Run as background daemon |
   148→
   149→## File Locations
   150→
   151→- **Config**: `~/.config/compliance/config.json`
   152→- **Stats**: `~/.config/compliance/stats.json`
   153→- **PID**: `~/.config/compliance/compliance.pid`
   154→- **Logs**: `~/.config/compliance/compliance.log`
   155→
   156→## How It Works
   157→
   158→1. **Inactivity detection**: The app monitors the mouse position
   159→2. **Threshold**: Only activates if you've been idle for more than 30s (configurable)
   160→3. **Micro-movement**: Moves the mouse 1-2 pixels in a random direction and returns
   161→4. **Randomization**: Interval varies ±30% to avoid detectable patterns
   162→5. **Statistics**: Tracks all jiggles and activity for monitoring
   163→
   164→## Troubleshooting
   165→
   166→### "Mouse jiggle failed"
   167→→ Accessibility permission not granted. See configuration section.
   168→
   169→### Status still goes to Away
   170→→ Reduce the interval: `compliance config set intervalMs 30000`
   171→→ Reduce the threshold: `compliance config set idleThresholdMs 10000`
   172→
   173→### Visible mouse movement
   174→→ Reduce pixels: `compliance config set movementPx 1`
   175→
   176→### Daemon won't start
   177→```bash
   178→# Check if already running
   179→compliance status
   180→
   181→# Check logs for errors
   182→compliance logs -n 50
   183→```
   184→
   185→### Reset everything
   186→```bash
   187→compliance config reset
   188→compliance stop
   189→```
   190→
   191→## Legal Notice
   192→
   193→This app is a technical tool for maintaining presence when you are genuinely working but physically away from the computer. Usage is the user's responsibility and should comply with your organization's policies.
   194→
   195→## License
   196→
   197→MIT
   198→

     6→  "version": "0.1.2",

    10→    "type": "git",

    14→  "bugs": {

    18→  "keywords": [

    70→    "prettier-plugin-organize-imports": "^4.3.0",

    72→    "terser": "^5.44.1",
    73→    "typescript": "^5.9.3",
