toolUseResult.originalFile:
name: Publish

on:
  push:
    tags:
      - 'v*'

permissions:
  contents: write
  id-token: write

jobs:
  publish:
    name: Publish to NPM
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 15

    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Validate tag format
        run: |
          TAG="${GITHUB_REF#refs/tags/}"
          if [[ ! "$TAG" =~ ^v[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+$ ]]; then
            echo "::error::Invalid tag format: $TAG (expected vX.Y.Z)"
            exit 1
          fi
          echo "VERSION=${TAG#v}" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          echo "TAG=$TAG" >> $GITHUB_ENV

      - name: Verify version matches tag
        run: |
          PKG_VERSION=$(jq -r '.version' package.json)
          if [ "$PKG_VERSION" != "$VERSION" ]; then
            echo "::error::Tag $TAG doesn't match package.json version $PKG_VERSION"
            exit 1
          fi

      - name: Setup Bun
        uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2

      - name: Setup NPM authentication
        run: |
          echo "//registry.npmjs.org/:_authToken=${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}" > ~/.npmrc
          echo "registry=https://registry.npmjs.org/" >> ~/.npmrc

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: bun install --frozen-lockfile

      - name: Build all packages
        run: bun run build

      - name: Type check
        run: bun run check

      - name: Test
        run: bun run test

      - name: Verify dist files exist
        run: |
          for pkg in tools cli ai-tools belt-setup prettier-plugin; do
            if [ ! -d "packages/$pkg/dist" ]; then
              echo "::error::Missing dist for packages/$pkg"
              exit 1
            fi
          done

      - name: Publish @belt/tools
        working-directory: packages/tools
        env:
          NPM_CONFIG_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}
        run: bun publish --access public

      - name: Publish @belt/cli
        working-directory: packages/cli
        env:
          NPM_CONFIG_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}
        run: bun publish --access public

      - name: Publish @belt/ai
        working-directory: packages/ai-tools
        env:
          NPM_CONFIG_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}
        run: bun publish --access public

      - name: Publish belt-setup
        working-directory: packages/belt-setup
        env:
          NPM_CONFIG_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}
        run: bun publish --access public

      - name: Publish @belt/prettier-plugin
        working-directory: packages/prettier-plugin
        env:
          NPM_CONFIG_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}
        run: bun publish --access public

      - name: Create GitHub Release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
        with:
          tag_name: ${{ env.TAG }}
          name: ${{ env.TAG }}
          generate_release_notes: true

# Belt Monorepo

TypeScript utility library with AI integration, CLI tools, and developer utilities.

## IMPORTANT (ALL RULES MUST BE FOLLOWED):
- THINK, PLAN, AND EXECUTE/WRITE CODE IN ENGLISH, ANSWER CHAT USING PORTUGUESE.
-  **ALL (EVEN SMALL) TASKS MUST BE DELIVERED PRODUCTION-READY!**:
  - ALL (EVEN SMALL) TASKS MUST BE DELIVERED PRODUCTION-READY - **ALL (EVEN SMALL) TASKS MUST BE DELIVERED PRODUCTION-READY!**
  - NO MOCKS IN CODE ARE ALLOWED - **ALL (EVEN SMALL) TASKS MUST BE DELIVERED PRODUCTION-READY!**
  - AGAIN: **ALL (EVEN SMALL) TASKS MUST BE DELIVERED PRODUCTION-READY!**
  - FULL TEST IS MANDATORY - **ALL (EVEN SMALL) TASKS MUST BE DELIVERED PRODUCTION-READY!**:
    - MANUAL, INTEGRATION AND UNIT TESTS ARE REQUIRED FOR EVERY TASK -  **ALL (EVEN SMALL) TASKS MUST BE DELIVERED PRODUCTION-READY!**

## Project Structure

```
belt/
├── .belt/
│   └── harness/         # Progress tracking and features
├── .claude/
│   ├── settings.json    # Claude Code hooks configuration
│   └── commands/        # Skill workflows (publish, quality)
└── packages/
    ├── tools/           # @belt/tools - TypeScript utilities (schema, state-machine, helpers)
    ├── ai-tools/        # @belt/ai - Claude Code integration (AgentRC, hooks)
    ├── cli/             # @belt/cli - CLI with harness system
    ├── belt-setup/      # belt-setup - Setup utility
    ├── prettier-plugin/ # @belt/prettier-plugin - Line-break formatting
    └── compliance/      # @belt/compliance - Activity monitor
```

## Tech Stack

- **Monorepo**: Bun + Turborepo 2.3.0
- **Build**: tsup 8.3.0
- **Linting/Formatting**: Biome 2.3.8
- **TypeScript**: 5.9.3 (strict mode)
- **Testing**: Vitest

## Packages

| Package | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| @belt/tools | Schema validation, state machine, helpers, type utilities |
| @belt/ai | Claude Code integration, AgentRC rules, hook runner |
| @belt/cli | CLI commands, harness system (agents, brainstorm, router) |
| belt-setup | Setup utility for initializing projects |
| @belt/prettier-plugin | Prettier plugin for line-break formatting |
| @belt/compliance | Activity monitor daemon |

## Development

```bash
# Install dependencies
bun install

# Build
bun run build

# Run tests
bun test

# Type check
bun run typecheck

# Lint
bun lint
```

## NPM Publishing

```bash
# Build and verify
bun run build && bun run typecheck

input.content:
# Publish Workflow

Execute this workflow to publish Belt packages to NPM.

## Pre-publish Checklist

1. Ensure all packages build successfully
2. Run type checks
3. Ensure @belt organization exists on npmjs.com
4. Verify all tests pass

## Quality Checks

```bash
# Build all packages
bun run build

# Type check
bun run typecheck

# Lint
bun lint

# Run tests
bun test
```

## Publish Order

Packages must be published in dependency order:

| Order | Package | Dependencies |
|-------|---------|--------------|
| 1 | @belt/tools | None |
| 2 | @belt/prettier-plugin | None |
| 3 | @belt/ai | @belt/tools |

## Publish Commands

```bash
# 1. Packages with no internal dependencies
cd packages/tools && bun publish --access public
cd packages/prettier-plugin && bun publish --access public

# 2. Packages with dependencies (after above are published)
cd packages/ai-tools && bun publish --access public
```

Note: `--access public` is only required on first publish of each package.

## Post-publish

```bash
# Create and push git tag
git tag v<version>
git push origin v<version>

# Update harness progress
# Edit .belt/harness/progress.txt to reflect published state
```

## Troubleshooting

- **"Cannot resolve workspace protocol"**: Run from package root, not dist/
- **"Access denied"**: Verify NPM login and org membership
- **"Package not found"**: Ensure dependencies are published first (see order above)
- **"E403 Forbidden"**: Check npm token permissions and package scope

input.content:
# Publishing Guide

This guide explains how to publish new versions of Belt packages to NPM.

## Quick Start

```bash
# 1. Ensure you're on main branch with clean working tree
git checkout main
git status  # Should be clean

# 2. Bump version (choose one)
bun run version:patch  # 0.0.987 → 0.0.988 (bug fixes)
bun run version:minor  # 0.0.987 → 0.1.0   (new features)
bun run version:major  # 0.0.987 → 1.0.0   (breaking changes)

# 3. Push commit and tags to trigger automated publishing
git push origin main --tags
```

That's it! GitHub Actions will automatically:
- Build all packages
- Run tests and type checks
- Publish to NPM
- Create a GitHub Release

## Semantic Versioning

We follow [Semantic Versioning 2.0.0](https://semver.org/):

| Version Type | When to Use | Example |
|--------------|-------------|---------|
| **Patch** (`x.y.Z`) | Bug fixes, documentation, internal changes | `0.0.987 → 0.0.988` |
| **Minor** (`x.Y.0`) | New features (backward compatible) | `0.0.987 → 0.1.0` |
| **Major** (`X.0.0`) | Breaking changes | `0.0.987 → 1.0.0` |

## The `version:*` Scripts

Located in `scripts/bump-version.sh`, these scripts:

### What They Do

1. ✅ Validate current version is valid semver (X.Y.Z)
2. ✅ Calculate new version based on bump type
3. ✅ Check that tag `vX.Y.Z` doesn't already exist
4. ✅ Update version in root `package.json`
5. ✅ Update version in ALL package `package.json` files:
   - `packages/tools/package.json`
   - `packages/ai-tools/package.json`
   - `packages/prettier-plugin/package.json`
6. ✅ Create git commit: `chore: release vX.Y.Z`
7. ✅ Create git tag: `vX.Y.Z`

### What They DON'T Do

- ❌ Push to remote (you must do this manually)
- ❌ Publish to NPM (GitHub Actions does this)
- ❌ Create GitHub Release (GitHub Actions does this)

## GitHub Actions Publish Workflow

When you push a tag matching `v*` pattern, the `.github/workflows/publish.yml` workflow runs:

### Workflow Steps

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Push tag v0.0.988] --> B[Validate tag format]
    B --> C[Verify version matches package.json]
    C --> D[Setup Bun]
    D --> E[Install dependencies]
    E --> F[Build all packages]
    F --> G[Type check]
    G --> H[Run tests]
    H --> I[Verify dist/ exists]
    I --> J[Publish @belt/tools with bun]
    J --> K[Publish @belt/ai with bun]
    K --> L[Publish @belt/prettier-plugin with bun]
    L --> M[Create GitHub Release]
```

**Note:** We use `bun publish` instead of `npm publish` to automatically resolve `workspace:*` dependencies to actual version numbers.

### Packages Published

The workflow publishes these packages to NPM:

| Package | NPM Name | Access |
|---------|----------|--------|
| tools | `@belt/tools` | public |
| ai-tools | `@belt/ai` | public |
| prettier-plugin | `@belt/prettier-plugin` | public |

### Authentication

The workflow requires an **NPM Access Token** configured as a GitHub Secret:

**⚠️ CRITICAL: NPM Token Setup Required**

Before publishing, you must configure the NPM token:

1. **Create NPM Granular Access Token** at https://www.npmjs.com/settings/[username]/tokens
2. **Add to GitHub Secrets** as `NPM_TOKEN` at https://github.com/antoniopresto/belt/settings/secrets/actions

See **[NPM_SETUP.md](./NPM_SETUP.md)** for complete setup instructions.

**Token Details:**
- Type: Granular Access Token (not classic)
- Expiration: 90 days maximum
- Permissions: Read and write on all `@belt/*` packages
- 2FA: Required by default

## Complete Publishing Example

### Step-by-Step Walkthrough

```bash
# Check current status
cd /Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/belt
git status
# On branch main
# Your branch is up to date with 'origin/main'.
# nothing to commit, working tree clean

# Check current version
cat package.json | grep version
#   "version": "0.0.987",

# Bump patch version
bun run version:patch

# Output:
# Current version: 0.0.987
# New version: 0.0.988
# Updated: package.json
# Updated: packages/ai-tools/package.json
# Updated: packages/prettier-plugin/package.json
# Updated: packages/tools/package.json
# [main abc1234] chore: release v0.0.988
#  4 files changed, 4 insertions(+), 4 deletions(-)
#
# Version bumped to 0.0.988
# Commit created
# Tag v0.0.988 created
#
# To publish, run:
#   git push origin main --tags

# Verify the commit
git log -1

# Push commit and tags
git push origin main --tags
```

### Monitor the Workflow

1. Go to GitHub Actions: https://github.com/antoniopresto/belt/actions
2. You should see a "Publish" workflow running
3. Wait for it to complete (~2-5 minutes)

### Verify Publication

```bash
# Check NPM versions (wait ~1 minute after workflow completes)
npm view @belt/tools version
npm view @belt/ai version
npm view @belt/prettier-plugin version

# All should return: 0.0.988
```

### Check GitHub Release

1. Go to: https://github.com/antoniopresto/belt/releases
2. You should see a new release: `v0.0.988`
3. Release notes are auto-generated from commits

## Troubleshooting

### Tag Already Exists

**Error:**
```
Error: Tag v0.0.988 already exists
```

**Solution:**
```bash
# Delete local tag
git tag -d v0.0.988

# Delete remote tag (if pushed)
git push origin :refs/tags/v0.0.988

# Try again
bun run version:patch
```

### Version Mismatch

**Error in GitHub Actions:**
```
Error: Tag v0.0.988 doesn't match package.json version 0.0.987
```

**Solution:**
This should never happen if you use the `version:*` scripts. If it does:

```bash
# Manually fix all package.json files
# Then:
git add package.json packages/*/package.json
git commit --amend --no-edit
git tag -f v0.0.988
git push origin main --tags --force
```

### NPM Publish Permission Denied

**Error in GitHub Actions:**
```
npm ERR! 403 Forbidden - PUT https://registry.npmjs.org/@belt/tools
```

**Solution:**
- Ensure you're a collaborator/owner on NPM organization `@belt`
- Verify NPM OIDC is configured correctly
- Contact repository owner for access

### Workflow Not Triggered

**Problem:** Pushed tags but workflow didn't run

**Check:**
```bash
# Ensure tag matches pattern v*
git tag -l | grep v0.0.988
# v0.0.988

# Ensure tag was pushed to remote
git ls-remote --tags origin | grep v0.0.988
# abc1234... refs/tags/v0.0.988
```

### Build/Test Failures

**Problem:** Workflow fails during build or test

**Solution:**
1. Run locally first:
   ```bash
   bun run build
   bun run check
   bun run test
   ```
2. Fix any errors
3. Commit fixes
4. Delete failed tag and retry:
   ```bash
   git tag -d v0.0.988
   git push origin :refs/tags/v0.0.988
   bun run version:patch
   git push origin main --tags
   ```

## Pre-Release Checklist

Before publishing, ensure:

- [ ] **NPM Token is configured** in GitHub Secrets (see [NPM_SETUP.md](./NPM_SETUP.md))
- [ ] All tests pass locally: `bun run test`
- [ ] Build succeeds: `bun run build`
- [ ] Type check passes: `bun run check`
- [ ] Linting passes: `bun lint`
- [ ] All changes are committed
- [ ] You're on the `main` branch
- [ ] Working tree is clean: `git status`
- [ ] You've pulled latest changes: `git pull`
- [ ] CHANGELOG.md is updated (optional but recommended)

## Rolling Back a Release

If you need to unpublish or rollback:

### Unpublish from NPM (within 72 hours)

```bash
# Unpublish specific version
npm unpublish @belt/tools@0.0.988
npm unpublish @belt/ai@0.0.988
npm unpublish @belt/prettier-plugin@0.0.988
```

### Delete GitHub Release

1. Go to: https://github.com/antoniopresto/belt/releases
2. Find the release
3. Click "Delete"

### Delete Git Tag

```bash
# Delete local tag
git tag -d v0.0.988

# Delete remote tag
git push origin :refs/tags/v0.0.988
```

### Revert Version Commit

```bash
# Revert the release commit
git revert HEAD
git push origin main
```

## Manual Publishing (Emergency Only)

If GitHub Actions is down, you can publish manually:

```bash
# Build all packages
bun run build

# Verify everything works
bun run check
bun run test

# Login to NPM (if not already logged in)
npm login

# Publish each package manually (using bun to resolve workspace:* dependencies)
cd packages/tools && bun publish --access public
cd ../ai-tools && bun publish --access public
cd ../prettier-plugin && bun publish --access public
```

**⚠️ Warning:** Manual publishing bypasses automated checks and should only be used in emergencies.

**Why use `bun publish` instead of `npm publish`?**
- Bun automatically resolves `workspace:*` dependencies to actual version numbers
- npm would publish with `workspace:*` which breaks installation for end users

## Best Practices

1. **Always use the `version:*` scripts** - Never manually edit version numbers
2. **Test before releasing** - Run full test suite locally
3. **Update CHANGELOG.md** - Document changes for users
4. **Use conventional commits** - Makes it easier to generate changelogs
5. **Release often** - Small, frequent releases are better than large ones
6. **Monitor the workflow** - Watch GitHub Actions to catch issues early
7. **Verify publication** - Check NPM after workflow completes

## Related Documentation

- **[NPM_SETUP.md](./NPM_SETUP.md)** - NPM token setup and troubleshooting (READ THIS FIRST!)
- [TESTING.md](./TESTING.md) - How to run tests
- [README.md](./README.md) - Project overview and development
- [CHANGELOG.md](./CHANGELOG.md) - Version history
- [GitHub Actions Workflows](./.github/workflows/) - CI/CD configuration

## Support

If you encounter issues:

1. **NPM Authentication errors** → See [NPM_SETUP.md](./NPM_SETUP.md)
2. Check this guide's troubleshooting section
3. Review GitHub Actions logs
4. Check NPM package status
5. Contact @antoniopresto for repository/NPM access issues

#   "version": "0.0.987",

# Bump patch version
bun run version:patch

# Output:
# Current version: 0.0.987
# New version: 0.0.988
# Updated: package.json
# Updated: packages/ai-tools/package.json
# Updated: packages/prettier-plugin/package.json
# Updated: packages/tools/package.json
# [main abc1234] chore: release v0.0.988
#  4 files changed, 4 insertions(+), 4 deletions(-)
#
# Version bumped to 0.0.988
# Commit created
# Tag v0.0.988 created
#
# To publish, run:
#   git push origin main --tags

# Verify the commit
git log -1

# Push commit and tags
git push origin main --tags
```

### Monitor the Workflow

1. Go to GitHub Actions: https://github.com/antoniopresto/belt/actions
2. You should see a "Publish" workflow running
3. Wait for it to complete (~2-5 minutes)

### Verify Publication

```bash
# Check NPM versions (wait ~1 minute after workflow completes)
npm view @belt/tools version
npm view @belt/ai version
npm view @belt/prettier-plugin version

# All should return: 0.0.988
```

### Check GitHub Release

1. Go to: https://github.com/antoniopresto/belt/releases
2. You should see a new release: `v0.0.988`
3. Release notes are auto-generated from commits

## Troubleshooting

### Tag Already Exists

**Error:**
```
Error: Tag v0.0.988 already exists
```

**Solution:**
```bash
# Delete local tag
git tag -d v0.0.988

# Delete remote tag (if pushed)
git push origin :refs/tags/v0.0.988

# Try again
bun run version:patch
```

### Version Mismatch

**Error in GitHub Actions:**
```
Error: Tag v0.0.988 doesn't match package.json version 0.0.987
```

**Solution:**
This should never happen if you use the `version:*` scripts. If it does:

```bash
# Manually fix all package.json files
# Then:
git add package.json packages/*/package.json
git commit --amend --no-edit
git tag -f v0.0.988
git push origin main --tags --force
```

### NPM Publish Permission Denied

**Error in GitHub Actions:**
```
npm ERR! 403 Forbidden - PUT https://registry.npmjs.org/@belt/tools
```

**Solution:**
- Ensure you're a collaborator/owner on NPM organization `@belt`
- Verify NPM OIDC is configured correctly
- Contact repository owner for access

### Workflow Not Triggered

**Problem:** Pushed tags but workflow didn't run

**Check:**
```bash
# Ensure tag matches pattern v*
git tag -l | grep v0.0.988
# v0.0.988

# Ensure tag was pushed to remote
git ls-remote --tags origin | grep v0.0.988
# abc1234... refs/tags/v0.0.988
```

### Build/Test Failures

**Problem:** Workflow fails during build or test

**Solution:**
1. Run locally first:
   ```bash
   bun run build
   bun run check
   bun run test
   ```
2. Fix any errors
3. Commit fixes
4. Delete failed tag and retry:
   ```bash
   git tag -d v0.0.988
   git push origin :refs/tags/v0.0.988
   bun run version:patch
   git push origin main --tags
   ```

## Pre-Release Checklist

Before publishing, ensure:

- [ ] **NPM Token is configured** in GitHub Secrets (see [NPM_SETUP.md](./NPM_SETUP.md))
- [ ] All tests pass locally: `bun run test`
- [ ] Build succeeds: `bun run build`
- [ ] Type check passes: `bun run check`
- [ ] Linting passes: `bun lint`
- [ ] All changes are committed
- [ ] You're on the `main` branch
- [ ] Working tree is clean: `git status`
- [ ] You've pulled latest changes: `git pull`
- [ ] CHANGELOG.md is updated (optional but recommended)

## Rolling Back a Release

If you need to unpublish or rollback:

### Unpublish from NPM (within 72 hours)

```bash
# Unpublish specific version
npm unpublish @belt/tools@0.0.988
npm unpublish @belt/ai@0.0.988
npm unpublish @belt/prettier-plugin@0.0.988
```

### Delete GitHub Release

1. Go to: https://github.com/antoniopresto/belt/releases
2. Find the release
3. Click "Delete"

### Delete Git Tag

```bash
# Delete local tag
git tag -d v0.0.988

# Delete remote tag
git push origin :refs/tags/v0.0.988
```

### Revert Version Commit

```bash
# Revert the release commit
git revert HEAD
git push origin main
```

## Manual Publishing (Emergency Only)

If GitHub Actions is down, you can publish manually:

```bash
# Build all packages
bun run build

# Verify everything works
bun run check
bun run test

# Login to NPM (if not already logged in)
npm login

# Publish each package manually (using bun to resolve workspace:* dependencies)
cd packages/tools && bun publish --access public
cd ../ai-tools && bun publish --access public
cd ../prettier-plugin && bun publish --access public
```

**⚠️ Warning:** Manual publishing bypasses automated checks and should only be used in emergencies.

**Why use `bun publish` instead of `npm publish`?**
- Bun automatically resolves `workspace:*` dependencies to actual version numbers
- npm would publish with `workspace:*` which breaks installation for end users

## Best Practices

1. **Always use the `version:*` scripts** - Never manually edit version numbers
2. **Test before releasing** - Run full test suite locally
3. **Update CHANGELOG.md** - Document changes for users
4. **Use conventional commits** - Makes it easier to generate changelogs
5. **Release often** - Small, frequent releases are better than large ones
6. **Monitor the workflow** - Watch GitHub Actions to catch issues early
7. **Verify publication** - Check NPM after workflow completes

## Related Documentation

- **[NPM_SETUP.md](./NPM_SETUP.md)** - NPM token setup and troubleshooting (READ THIS FIRST!)
- [TESTING.md](./TESTING.md) - How to run tests
- [README.md](./README.md) - Project overview and development
- [CHANGELOG.md](./CHANGELOG.md) - Version history
- [GitHub Actions Workflows](./.github/workflows/) - CI/CD configuration

## Support

If you encounter issues:

1. **NPM Authentication errors** → See [NPM_SETUP.md](./NPM_SETUP.md)
2. Check this guide's troubleshooting section
3. Review GitHub Actions logs
4. Check NPM package status
5. Contact @antoniopresto for repository/NPM access issues

# Publishing Guide

This guide explains how to publish new versions of Belt packages to NPM.

## Quick Start

```bash
# 1. Ensure you're on main branch with clean working tree
git checkout main
git status  # Should be clean

# 2. Bump version (choose one)
bun run version:patch  # 0.0.987 → 0.0.988 (bug fixes)
bun run version:minor  # 0.0.987 → 0.1.0   (new features)
bun run version:major  # 0.0.987 → 1.0.0   (breaking changes)

# 3. Push commit and tags to trigger automated publishing
git push origin main --tags
```

That's it! GitHub Actions will automatically:
- Build all packages
- Run tests and type checks
- Publish to NPM
- Create a GitHub Release

## Semantic Versioning

We follow [Semantic Versioning 2.0.0](https://semver.org/):

| Version Type | When to Use | Example |
|--------------|-------------|---------|
| **Patch** (`x.y.Z`) | Bug fixes, documentation, internal changes | `0.0.987 → 0.0.988` |
| **Minor** (`x.Y.0`) | New features (backward compatible) | `0.0.987 → 0.1.0` |
| **Major** (`X.0.0`) | Breaking changes | `0.0.987 → 1.0.0` |

## The `version:*` Scripts

Located in `scripts/bump-version.sh`, these scripts:

### What They Do

1. ✅ Validate current version is valid semver (X.Y.Z)
2. ✅ Calculate new version based on bump type
3. ✅ Check that tag `vX.Y.Z` doesn't already exist
4. ✅ Update version in root `package.json`
5. ✅ Update version in ALL package `package.json` files:
   - `packages/tools/package.json`
   - `packages/cli/package.json`
   - `packages/ai-tools/package.json`
   - `packages/belt-setup/package.json`
   - `packages/prettier-plugin/package.json`
6. ✅ Create git commit: `chore: release vX.Y.Z`
7. ✅ Create git tag: `vX.Y.Z`

### What They DON'T Do

- ❌ Push to remote (you must do this manually)
- ❌ Publish to NPM (GitHub Actions does this)
- ❌ Create GitHub Release (GitHub Actions does this)

## GitHub Actions Publish Workflow

When you push a tag matching `v*` pattern, the `.github/workflows/publish.yml` workflow runs:

### Workflow Steps

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Push tag v0.0.988] --> B[Validate tag format]
    B --> C[Verify version matches package.json]
    C --> D[Setup Bun]
    D --> E[Install dependencies]
    E --> F[Build all packages]
    F --> G[Type check]
    G --> H[Run tests]
    H --> I[Verify dist/ exists]
    I --> J[Publish @belt/tools with bun]
    J --> K[Publish @belt/cli with bun]
    K --> L[Publish @belt/ai with bun]
    L --> M[Publish belt-setup with bun]
    M --> N[Publish @belt/prettier-plugin with bun]
    N --> O[Create GitHub Release]
```

**Note:** We use `bun publish` instead of `npm publish` to automatically resolve `workspace:*` dependencies to actual version numbers.

### Packages Published

The workflow publishes these packages to NPM:

| Package | NPM Name | Access |
|---------|----------|--------|
| tools | `@belt/tools` | public |
| cli | `@belt/cli` | public |
| ai-tools | `@belt/ai` | public |
| belt-setup | `belt-setup` | public |
| prettier-plugin | `@belt/prettier-plugin` | public |

### Authentication

The workflow requires an **NPM Access Token** configured as a GitHub Secret:

**⚠️ CRITICAL: NPM Token Setup Required**

Before publishing, you must configure the NPM token:

1. **Create NPM Granular Access Token** at https://www.npmjs.com/settings/[username]/tokens
2. **Add to GitHub Secrets** as `NPM_TOKEN` at https://github.com/antoniopresto/belt/settings/secrets/actions

See **[NPM_SETUP.md](./NPM_SETUP.md)** for complete setup instructions.

**Token Details:**
- Type: Granular Access Token (not classic)
- Expiration: 90 days maximum
- Permissions: Read and write on all `@belt/*` packages
- 2FA: Required by default

## Complete Publishing Example

### Step-by-Step Walkthrough

```bash
# Check current status
cd /Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/belt
git status
# On branch main
# Your branch is up to date with 'origin/main'.
# nothing to commit, working tree clean

# Check current version
cat package.json | grep version
#   "version": "0.0.987",

# Bump patch version
bun run version:patch

# Output:
# Current version: 0.0.987
# New version: 0.0.988
# Updated: package.json
# Updated: packages/ai-tools/package.json
# Updated: packages/belt-setup/package.json
# Updated: packages/cli/package.json
# Updated: packages/prettier-plugin/package.json
# Updated: packages/tools/package.json
# [main abc1234] chore: release v0.0.988
#  6 files changed, 6 insertions(+), 6 deletions(-)
#
# Version bumped to 0.0.988
# Commit created
# Tag v0.0.988 created
#
# To publish, run:
#   git push origin main --tags

# Verify the commit
git log -1
# commit abc1234...
# Author: Your Name <your.email@example.com>
# Date:   Fri Jan 10 12:34:56 2026 -0300
#
#     chore: release v0.0.988

# Verify the tag
git tag -l | tail -1
# v0.0.988

# Push commit and tags
git push origin main --tags
# Enumerating objects: 15, done.
# Counting objects: 100% (15/15), done.
# ...
# To github.com:antoniopresto/belt.git
#    abc1233..abc1234  main -> main
#  * [new tag]         v0.0.988 -> v0.0.988
```

### Monitor the Workflow

1. Go to GitHub Actions: https://github.com/antoniopresto/belt/actions
2. You should see a "Publish" workflow running
3. Wait for it to complete (~2-5 minutes)

### Verify Publication

```bash
# Check NPM versions (wait ~1 minute after workflow completes)
npm view @belt/tools version
npm view @belt/cli version
npm view @belt/ai version
npm view belt-setup version
npm view @belt/prettier-plugin version

# All should return: 0.0.988
```

### Check GitHub Release

1. Go to: https://github.com/antoniopresto/belt/releases
2. You should see a new release: `v0.0.988`
3. Release notes are auto-generated from commits

## Troubleshooting

### Tag Already Exists

Error: Tag v0.0.988 already exists
```

**Solution:**
```bash
# Delete local tag
git tag -d v0.0.988

# Delete remote tag (if pushed)

# Try again
bun run version:patch
```

### Version Mismatch

**Error in GitHub Actions:**

```

**Solution:**
This should never happen if you use the `version:*` scripts. If it does:

```bash
# Manually fix all package.json files
# Then:
git add package.json packages/*/package.json
git commit --amend --no-edit
git tag -f v0.0.988
git push origin main --tags --force
```

### NPM Publish Permission Denied

**Error in GitHub Actions:**
```
npm ERR! 403 Forbidden - PUT https://registry.npmjs.org/@belt/tools
```

**Solution:**
- Ensure you're a collaborator/owner on NPM organization `@belt`
- Verify NPM OIDC is configured correctly
- Contact repository owner for access

### Workflow Not Triggered

**Problem:** Pushed tags but workflow didn't run

**Check:**
```bash
# Ensure tag matches pattern v*
git tag -l | grep v0.0.988
# v0.0.988

# Ensure tag was pushed to remote
git ls-remote --tags origin | grep v0.0.988
# abc1234... refs/tags/v0.0.988
```

### Build/Test Failures

**Problem:** Workflow fails during build or test

**Solution:**
1. Run locally first:
   ```bash
   bun run build
   bun run check
   bun run test
   ```
2. Fix any errors
3. Commit fixes
4. Delete failed tag and retry:
   ```bash
   git tag -d v0.0.988
   git push origin :refs/tags/v0.0.988
   bun run version:patch
   git push origin main --tags
   ```

## Pre-Release Checklist

Before publishing, ensure:

- [ ] **NPM Token is configured** in GitHub Secrets (see [NPM_SETUP.md](./NPM_SETUP.md))
- [ ] All tests pass locally: `bun run test`
- [ ] Build succeeds: `bun run build`
- [ ] Type check passes: `bun run check`
- [ ] Linting passes: `bun lint`

- [ ] CHANGELOG.md is updated (optional but recommended)

## Rolling Back a Release

If you need to unpublish or rollback:

### Unpublish from NPM (within 72 hours)

```bash
# Unpublish specific version
npm unpublish @belt/tools@0.0.988
npm unpublish @belt/cli@0.0.988
npm unpublish @belt/ai@0.0.988
npm unpublish belt-setup@0.0.988
npm unpublish @belt/prettier-plugin@0.0.988
```

### Delete GitHub Release

1. Go to: https://github.com/antoniopresto/belt/releases
2. Find the release
3. Click "Delete"

### Delete Git Tag

```bash

# Delete remote tag
git push origin :refs/tags/v0.0.988
```

### Revert Version Commit

```bash

```

## Manual Publishing (Emergency Only)

If GitHub Actions is down, you can publish manually:

```bash

# Verify everything works
bun run check

input.content:
# Testing Guide

## Test Structure

This monorepo uses a clear separation between unit tests and integration/e2e tests:

### Test File Naming Conventions

- **Unit Tests**: `*.test.ts` or `*.spec.ts`
  - Fast, isolated tests
  - No external dependencies (APIs, databases, etc.)
  - Run in CI/CD pipeline

- **Integration Tests**: `*.integration.test.ts`
  - Tests that interact with external services
  - May require Claude Code, Ollama, or other external APIs
  - Run locally only (not in CI)

- **E2E Tests**: `*.e2e.test.ts`
  - End-to-end tests of complete workflows
  - May spawn processes, use real CLIs
  - Run locally only (not in CI)

## Available Test Commands

### Root Level (all packages)

```bash
# Run unit tests only (CI mode)
bun run test

# Run all tests including integration/e2e
bun run test:all

# Watch mode for unit tests
bun run test:watch
```

### Package Level

Each package supports the following scripts:

```bash
# Unit tests only (excludes integration/e2e)
bun run test

# Watch mode for unit tests
bun run test:watch

# Integration/e2e tests only
bun run test:integration

# All tests (unit + integration + e2e)
bun run test:all

# Coverage report
bun run coverage
```

## Test Organization by Package

### @belt/tools
- **Unit Tests**: `src/schema/lib/__tests__/type.spec.ts`
- **Integration Tests**: None

### @belt/prettier-plugin
- **Unit Tests**: None (yet)
- **Integration Tests**: None

### @belt/ai
- **Unit Tests**: None (yet)
- **Integration Tests**:
  - `src/__tests__/integration/runner.integration.test.ts`
  - `src/__tests__/integration/setup.integration.test.ts`
  - `src/__tests__/integration/claude-code.integration.test.ts`

## CI/CD Behavior

The GitHub Actions CI workflow (`bun run test`) only runs unit tests:

- ✅ Fast execution
- ✅ No external dependencies required
- ✅ Reliable and deterministic
- ❌ Does not run integration/e2e tests

## Running Integration Tests Locally

Integration and e2e tests require external dependencies:

### Prerequisites for Integration Tests

- **Claude Code** integration tests:
  - Requires Claude Code CLI installed
  - May need valid API credentials

### Running Specific Test Suites

```bash
# Run all integration tests in @belt/ai
cd packages/ai-tools
bun run test:integration

# Run a specific integration test file
cd packages/ai-tools
bun run vitest run src/__tests__/integration/runner.integration.test.ts
```

## Writing New Tests

### Unit Test Template

```typescript
// src/utils/__tests__/helper.test.ts
import { describe, it, expect } from 'vitest';
import { myHelper } from '../helper';

describe('myHelper', () => {
  it('should do something', () => {
    expect(myHelper('input')).toBe('expected');
  });
});
```

### Integration Test Template

```typescript
// src/__tests__/integration/feature.integration.test.ts
import { describe, it, expect, beforeAll } from 'vitest';

describe('Feature Integration', () => {
  beforeAll(() => {
    // Setup: ensure external services are available
  });

  it('should integrate with external service', async () => {
    // Test that requires external API/service
  });
});
```

## Test Configuration

Each package has a `vitest.config.ts`:

```typescript
import { defineConfig } from 'vite';

export default defineConfig({
  test: {
    globals: true,
    watch: false,
    testTimeout: 10000, // Adjust per package needs
  },
});
```

## Troubleshooting

### Tests Pass Locally but Fail in CI

- Check if the test depends on external services
- Ensure the test file follows naming conventions (`*.test.ts` for CI)

### Integration Tests Not Running

- Verify file naming: `*.integration.test.ts` or `*.e2e.test.ts`
- Run with `bun run test:integration` or `bun run test:all`

### No Tests Found

- Check file location matches vitest include patterns
- Verify test file extensions (`.test.ts` or `.spec.ts`)

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Testing Guide

## Test Structure

This monorepo uses a clear separation between unit tests and integration/e2e tests:

### Test File Naming Conventions

- **Unit Tests**: `*.test.ts` or `*.spec.ts`
  - Fast, isolated tests
  - No external dependencies (APIs, databases, etc.)
  - Run in CI/CD pipeline

- **Integration Tests**: `*.integration.test.ts`
  - Tests that interact with external services
  - May require Claude Code, Ollama, or other external APIs
  - Run locally only (not in CI)

- **E2E Tests**: `*.e2e.test.ts`
  - End-to-end tests of complete workflows
  - May spawn processes, use real CLIs
  - Run locally only (not in CI)

## Available Test Commands

### Root Level (all packages)

```bash
# Run unit tests only (CI mode)
bun run test

# Run all tests including integration/e2e
bun run test:all

# Watch mode for unit tests
bun run test:watch
```

### Package Level

Each package supports the following scripts:

```bash
# Unit tests only (excludes integration/e2e)
bun run test

# Watch mode for unit tests
bun run test:watch

# Integration/e2e tests only
bun run test:integration

# All tests (unit + integration + e2e)
bun run test:all

# Coverage report
bun run coverage
```

## Test Organization by Package

### @belt/tools
- **Unit Tests**: `src/schema/lib/__tests__/type.spec.ts`
- **Integration Tests**: None

### @belt/prettier-plugin
- **Unit Tests**: None (yet)
- **Integration Tests**: None

### @belt/ai
- **Unit Tests**: None (yet)
- **Integration Tests**:
  - `src/__tests__/integration/runner.integration.test.ts`
  - `src/__tests__/integration/setup.integration.test.ts`
  - `src/__tests__/integration/claude-code.integration.test.ts`

### @belt/cli
- **Unit Tests**: `src/__tests__/harness/download-manager.test.ts`
- **E2E Tests**:
  - `src/__tests__/e2e/agents.e2e.test.ts`
  - `src/__tests__/e2e/brainstorm-phases.e2e.test.ts`
  - `src/__tests__/e2e/brainstorm.e2e.test.ts`
  - `src/__tests__/e2e/cli.e2e.test.ts`
  - `src/__tests__/e2e/hook.e2e.test.ts`
  - `src/__tests__/e2e/init.e2e.test.ts`
  - `src/__tests__/e2e/ollama.e2e.test.ts`
  - `src/__tests__/e2e/persistence.e2e.test.ts`
  - `src/__tests__/e2e/router.e2e.test.ts`
  - `src/__tests__/e2e/setup.e2e.test.ts`

### belt-setup
- **Unit Tests**: `src/__tests__/cli.test.ts`
- **Integration Tests**: None

## CI/CD Behavior

The GitHub Actions CI workflow (`bun run test`) only runs unit tests:

- ✅ Fast execution
- ✅ No external dependencies required
- ✅ Reliable and deterministic
- ❌ Does not run integration/e2e tests

## Running Integration Tests Locally

Integration and e2e tests require external dependencies:

### Prerequisites for Integration Tests

- **Claude Code** integration tests:
  - Requires Claude Code CLI installed
  - May need valid API credentials

- **Ollama** integration tests:
  - Requires Ollama installed and running
  - Models must be downloaded

### Running Specific Test Suites

```bash
# Run all integration tests in @belt/ai
cd packages/ai-tools
bun run test:integration

# Run all e2e tests in @belt/cli
cd packages/cli
bun run test:integration

# Run a specific integration test file
cd packages/ai-tools
bun run vitest run src/__tests__/integration/runner.integration.test.ts
```

## Writing New Tests

### Unit Test Template

```typescript
// src/utils/__tests__/helper.test.ts
import { describe, it, expect } from 'vitest';
import { myHelper } from '../helper';

describe('myHelper', () => {
  it('should do something', () => {
    expect(myHelper('input')).toBe('expected');
  });
});
```

### Integration Test Template

```typescript
// src/__tests__/integration/feature.integration.test.ts
import { describe, it, expect, beforeAll } from 'vitest';

describe('Feature Integration', () => {
  beforeAll(() => {
    // Setup: ensure external services are available
  });

  it('should integrate with external service', async () => {
    // Test that requires external API/service
  });
});
```

## Test Configuration

Each package has a `vitest.config.ts`:

```typescript
import { defineConfig } from 'vite';

export default defineConfig({
  test: {
    globals: true,
    watch: false,
    testTimeout: 10000, // Adjust per package needs
  },
});
```

## Troubleshooting

### Tests Pass Locally but Fail in CI

- Check if the test depends on external services
- Ensure the test file follows naming conventions (`*.test.ts` for CI)

### Integration Tests Not Running

- Verify file naming: `*.integration.test.ts` or `*.e2e.test.ts`
- Run with `bun run test:integration` or `bun run test:all`

### No Tests Found

- Check file location matches vitest include patterns
- Verify test file extensions (`.test.ts` or `.spec.ts`)

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Changelog

All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.

The format is based on [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.1.0/),
and this project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html).

## [0.1.0] - 2024-12-29

### Added

- **@belt/core**: Plugin system with tapable hooks
  - Lifecycle hooks: initialize, beforeCheck, checkResults, afterCheck
  - Command hooks: registerCommands, beforeCommand, afterCommand
  - Utility hooks: log, shutdown

- **@belt/plugin-typecheck**: TypeScript diagnostic filtering
  - Include/exclude patterns for file filtering
  - Configurable tsconfig path
  - Structured output with file locations

- **@belt/plugin-sync-types**: Type declaration generation
  - Discover imports from external projects
  - Generate `declare module` statements
  - Recursive type extraction with configurable depth

- **@belt/cli**: Command-line interface
  - React Ink TUI for interactive mode
  - Commands: typecheck, sync-types, check, ui
  - meow for argument parsing

### Infrastructure

- pnpm 9.14.2 + Turborepo 2.3.0 monorepo
- Biome 2.3.8 for linting and formatting
- TypeScript 5.9.3 with strict mode
- tsup 8.3.0 for ESM builds
- Husky 9.1.7 for git hooks
